About the role

Mater's Digital Technology & Information team is seeking a Digital Support Specialist to join our Gold Coast based team in a full-time, 12 month maximum-term position based at our Gold Coast Office.

This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in supporting Mater's continued growth, including major workforce expansion across Mater Hospital Springfield and the Gold Coast. Working within a fast-paced healthcare environment, you'll provide expert technical support, resolve complex IT issues, and help ensure our people have the digital tools they need to deliver exceptional patient care.

Salary range: $46.96 - $51.73 per hour plus superannuation and access to not-for-profit salary packaging up to $15,900  + access to many staff benefits! 

What you’ll be doing

As a Digital Support Specialist, you'll provide advanced technical support while contributing to the ongoing success and improvement of Mater's Digital Service Centre.

In this role, you will:

  • Investigate and resolve complex desktop, application, hardware, and technology-related issues
  • Provide Level 1 and Level 2 support across desktop, audio-visual, telephony, networking, and mobile technologies
  • Deliver support via phone, digital channels, and face-to-face interactions
  • Install, configure, and upgrade IT equipment including computers, printers, AV equipment, and other technology devices
  • Assist with system upgrades, technology deployments, and approved change activities
  • Maintain accurate documentation, knowledge articles, and service records
  • Support IT asset management processes and equipment lifecycle activities
  • Provide technical guidance and mentoring to Digital Support Officers and Service Centre team members
  • Collaborate with specialist technical teams to identify opportunities for service improvement
  • Participate in an after-hours on-call support roster

About you

You are a customer-focused IT professional who enjoys solving complex technical problems and supporting people in a busy, service-driven environment. You bring strong troubleshooting capability, excellent communication skills, and a commitment to delivering high-quality support.

You will bring:

  • Certificate II or III in Information Technology, or equivalent industry experience
  • ITIL Foundation certification (Version 3 or 4)
  • Minimum four years' experience in a Service Desk, Desktop Support, or IT Support role
  • Experience supporting end users within large and complex organisations
  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft operating systems, Active Directory, networking, desktop hardware, and audio-visual technologies
  • Demonstrated ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues across a broad range of technologies
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise competing demands
  • A collaborative approach and willingness to support and mentor team members
  • Relevant tertiary qualifications and industry certifications (such as A+, Network+, or Microsoft certifications) will be highly regarded

Why join us?

Mater is Queensland’s largest not-for-profit healthcare provider, delivering exceptional care to nearly 700,000 patients each year across 11 (soon to be 12) hospitals. We’re leaders in healthcare, education,  fundraising & philanthropy and research — and it’s our people who make the difference.

We’re looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help shape the future of care. At Mater, you’ll be part of a purpose-driven organisation that values innovation, compassion, and excellence.

  • Increase your take-home pay with generous salary packaging options, including meal and entertainment benefits
  • Grow your career with access to industry-leading training and professional development through Mater Education
  • Wellbeing support through our Employee Assistance Program and on-site pastoral care teams
  • Access a range of health and wellbeing services with a corporate discount on private health insurance
  • Exclusive discounts across dining, retail, travel, accommodation (just to name a few!)
  • Stay active with unlimited access to discounted gyms via the Fitness Passport program
  • Convenient, discounted on-site parking
  • Enjoy great food with reduced prices at our campus cafés and coffee spots
  • Join Queensland’s Largest and Most Innovative Not-for-Profit Healthcare Team

Be part of something bigger. Make your move with Mater. #GETBETTERWITHMATER

Applications close Wednesday the 8th of July 2026 –please note, we may contact applicants prior to the closing date, please do not hesitate to APPLY NOW.

Direct applications only — we are not engaging recruitment agencies at this time. All successful candidates may be required to complete pre‑employment checks (including criminal history, working rights, medical and working with children checks).

Mater offers a safe, supportive, flexible and inclusive workplace where everyone is valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and abilities. Visit our Reconciliation webpage. 

Mater ensures all children have an environment suitable for their safety and wellbeing through proper supervision and safeguarding practices.

About the role

Mater Private Hospital Gold Coast is hiring a Digital Facilities Partner to join their team. In this role you will provide both leadership and technical expertise to drive the effective delivery of IT services at Mater Private Hospital Gold Coast.

Leading a small, dedicated front-line team, you will offer guidance and operational oversight to ensure exceptional customer support. Your role is pivotal in fostering scalable, reliable, and high-quality IT support that consistently meets the evolving needs at Mater.

This is a permanent, full-time position based on the Gold Coast.

Salary range: $54.75 - $59.38 plus superannuation and access to not-for-profit salary packaging up to $15,900  + access to many staff benefits! 

What you’ll be doing

  • Serve as a subject matter expert across digital, audio-visual, biomedical, and built environment technologies
  • Manage work queues, allocate resources, and provide hands-on support during high-demand periods
  • Oversee IT asset management, ensuring compliance with processes and guidelines
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with site leadership through clear, consistent communication
  • Lead knowledge management initiatives, including mentoring staff and maintaining self-service resources
  • Support incident, problem, and change management activities, including after-hours on-call support
  • Participate in root cause analysis and post-incident reviews to drive continuous improvement
  • Contribute to technical design, escalate complex issues, and provide Level 3 support
  • Identify training needs, deliver development programs, and maintain the Service Centre skills matrix
  • Coordinate customer feedback surveys, analyse results, and recommend service enhancements

About you      

  • Relevant tertiary degree in IT or related field
  • ITIL V3 or V4 Foundation certification required
  • Additional certifications such as A+, Network+, or Microsoft Certified Professional advantageous
  • Minimum 3 years’ experience in digital service delivery or corporate IT support
  • Proven experience supporting service desk, audio-visual, and desktop environments in large, complex organisations
  • Strong ability to provide leadership and mentor a small team
  • Ability to train and support advanced hardware and software troubleshooting
  • Commitment to delivering service excellence and fostering team development

Why join us?

Mater is Queensland’s largest not-for-profit healthcare provider, delivering exceptional care to nearly 700,000 patients each year across 11 (soon to be 12) hospitals. We’re leaders in healthcare, education,  fundraising & philanthropy and research — and it’s our people who make the difference.

We’re looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help shape the future of care. At Mater, you’ll be part of a purpose-driven organisation that values innovation, compassion, and excellence.

  • Increase your take-home pay with generous salary packaging options, including meal and entertainment benefits
  • Grow your career with access to industry-leading training and professional development through Mater Education
  • Wellbeing support through our Employee Assistance Program and on-site pastoral care teams
  • Access a range of health and wellbeing services with a corporate discount on private health insurance
  • Exclusive discounts across dining, retail, travel, accommodation (just to name a few!)
  • Stay active with unlimited access to discounted gyms via the Fitness Passport program
  • Convenient, discounted on-site parking
  • Enjoy great food with reduced prices at our campus cafés and coffee spots
  • Join Queensland’s Largest and Most Innovative Not-for-Profit Healthcare Team

Be part of something bigger. Make your move with Mater. #GETBETTERWITHMATER

About Buildsafe

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide. Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role
We're looking for an
Infrastructure & Systems Administrator to join our IT team, working alongside our Senior Infrastructure Administrator and reporting to the CIO. You'll play a key role in supporting and evolving the infrastructure that powers both Buildsafe and BuildCam.

This is a hands-on role combining enterprise systems administration with physical infrastructure deployment. From commissioning servers, expanding storage and configuring networks to managing Windows Server, Docker, IIS and Microsoft 365 environments, no two days are the same.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build, deploy and maintain enterprise server, storage and network infrastructure.

  • Administer Windows Server, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, IIS and Microsoft 365.

  • Deploy and manage Docker containers and hosted applications.

  • Monitor infrastructure performance, security, backups and disaster recovery.

  • Configure and support switches, firewalls, VPNs and networking equipment.

  • Troubleshoot infrastructure issues and support branch deployments.

  • Contribute to infrastructure projects, automation, documentation and continuous improvement.

AI-First Culture

AI is embedded in how we work. We encourage our IT team to use AI to automate repetitive tasks, troubleshoot faster, generate documentation, develop scripts and improve operational efficiency. We're looking for someone who enjoys learning, embraces new technology and is always looking for smarter ways to work.

About You

You'll ideally have:

  • Permanent working rights in Australia (essential)

  • 3+ years' experience in Infrastructure or Systems Administration.

  • Strong Windows Server, Active Directory, DNS and Group Policy experience.

  • Experience with IIS, Docker and Microsoft 365 administration.

  • Hands-on experience with enterprise server hardware and NAS/storage platforms (Synology highly regarded).

  • Solid networking knowledge (switches, VLANs, VPNs and firewalls).

  • PowerShell or scripting experience.

  • Strong troubleshooting, documentation and organisational skills.

  • A passion for AI, automation and continuous improvement.

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

Join a scaling division backed by an established national brand, where your contributions genuinely make an impact.

  • Permanent full-time role – salary paid weekly

  • Office based role, Monday to Friday

  • Corporate uniform allowance

  • Fortnightly professional car cleaning on site

  • Regular team bonding experiences

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

  • Discounted gym memberships through Fitness Passport

  • A culture that values innovation, safety, and collaboration

About Us

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide.

Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role

Our BuildCam camera system provides a flexible and reliable solution for site monitoring. With 24/7 supervision of residential construction sites, our customers and stakeholders can track developments in real time.

In this newly created role, we are seeking an experienced IT Support Officer to join our expanding BuildCam team. This position plays a key role in maintaining and supporting BuildCam’s technology and IT infrastructure. You’ll be responsible for the setup, troubleshooting, and monitoring of BuildCam systems, as well as footage and data management, software and hardware updates, and both customer and internal support.

The successful candidate will provide integral support to all aspects of BuildCam’s IT systems and services, both onsite and remotely. This position is hands-on, dynamic, and suited to someone who thrives in a problem-solving environment and enjoys working both independently and as part of a collaborative team.

Your Core Responsibilities

  • Provide onsite and remote technical support to internal teams and clients

  • Set up, configure, and troubleshoot BuildCam camera systems, modems, and solar monitoring equipment

  • Conduct quality assurance checks and ensure systems meet performance standards

  • Manage system updates, maintenance, and asset tracking

  • Monitor power, data, and connectivity performance across all BuildCam systems

  • Provide guidance and technical support via phone, email, or chat

  • Assist in continuous improvement by refining processes and identifying optimisation opportunities

About You

You’ll have recent experience working in a similar IT support role, with relevant qualifications in information technology or a related discipline. You’re someone who can work within defined timeframes, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate effectively with internal stakeholders to achieve great outcomes.

You’ll also bring:

  • Experience on a Service Desk or as an IT Support Officer

  • Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills (hardware, software, and networks)

  • Basic knowledge of server and networking hardware

  • Understanding of network setup, connectivity, and troubleshooting

  • Experience conducting quality assurance checks on technical systems

  • Ability to monitor system performance, data integrity, and security alerts

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills

  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Windows server and desktop operating systems

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

Join a scaling division backed by an established national brand, where your contributions genuinely make an impact.

  • Permanent full-time role – salary paid weekly

  • Monday to Friday, officed based

  • Corporate uniform allowance

  • Fortnightly professional car cleaning on site

  • Regular team bonding experiences

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

  • Discounted gym memberships through Fitness Passport

  • A culture that values innovation, safety, and collaboration

Ohana for Youth is a growing, values-driven organisation supporting young people who have disengaged from mainstream schooling. Through our education brands Arcadia College, Arcadia College (Distance Education), Ohana College and Ohana Academy, we provide safe, future-focused learning environments that help students reconnect and thrive.

As we continue to grow, we are seeking an experienced ICT Technician to provide exceptional technical support and maintain high system standards across our campuses. We will require an ICT Technician to be based at Varsity Lakes and a second ICT Technician to be based at Ohana College, Meadowbrook. Although based at these locations, ICT Technicians may be required to travel to and work from either our Logan locations or our Gold Coast locations at Varsity Lakes or Robina. 

Why Join Ohana for Youth?

  • Make a meaningful difference in the lives of young people

  • Join a supportive, purpose-led team

  • PBI salary packaging through SPA (Charity / Not-for-Profit status) – take home more pay and reduce tax

  • Employee Assistance Program for staff and families

  • Fitness Passport available

  • Opportunities for professional development 

Our Commitment to Child Safety

Ohana for Youth is committed to promoting a safe, inclusive and culturally responsive environment for all students, ensuring their safety and wellbeing is prioritised in every aspect of the organisations operations. We recognise our fundamental duty of care and are fully committed to the safety and wellbeing of all children and young people in our community. This responsibility is shared by every member of our organisation – leaders, staff, volunteers and contractors – and extends across all aspects of school life and operations.

Applications will be assessed as they are received and Ohana for Youth reserves the right to fill this position prior to the closing date.


Reporting directly to the IT Projects Lead & ICT Team Leader, you will play a hands-on role in providing a high level of ICT support to our staff, students, and community stakeholders. Operating within an organization-wide Apple device and Google Workspace environment, you will manage helpdesk tickets, maintain hardware, and ensure our network infrastructure runs seamlessly.

Your key responsibilities will include:

  • Helpdesk Support: Managing and resolving front desk queries, incoming calls, and helpdesk emails efficiently.

  • Hardware & Systems Maintenance: Troubleshooting, repairing, and installing computing equipment, peripherals, and PA/audiovisual systems.

  • Network & Software Support: Supporting local area networks, server infrastructure, wireless access points, and VoIP phone systems, while applying routine system updates and patches.

  • User & Asset Management: Handling user account creation/deletion and accurately keeping track of the organization’s ICT asset registers.

  • Collaborative Support: Partnering with teachers and staff to effectively implement technology in the classroom, with a willingness to travel to other campuses for on-site support when operational needs arise.


To be successful in this role, you will bring a customer-focused attitude alongside solid technical troubleshooting capabilities.

Requirements:

  • Essential: A current and valid Paid Working with Children Blue Card.

  • Experience: Proven experience working within an IT helpdesk environment with a strong practical understanding of Apple iOS and OS X operating systems.

  • Technical Skills: Sound troubleshooting skills across hardware, software applications, and peripheral devices.

  • Communication: Exceptional people skills, strong telephone etiquette, and the ability to work cooperatively within a dynamic team setting.

  • Desirable: A relevant qualification (Diploma or Degree) in ICT (or working towards one), and familiarity with Mobile Device Management (MDM), Microsoft systems, or Google Workspace.

jobs.job_title: Software Test Automation and DevOps Engineer
jobs.job_description:
An exciting opportunity has become available to join Siemens as a Software Test Automation & DevOps Engineer within our expanding OMVS R&D Team.

This newly created position, based in Yatala QLD, comes at a pivotal time for the business, with continued growth, strong project demand, and ongoing investment in innovative energy infrastructure technologies. Reporting directly to the OMVS R&D Manager, you will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high-quality software solutions across our medium voltage power distribution and protection product portfolio.

This is a hands-on technical role offering exposure to a broad range of technologies including:

  • Embedded firmware for protection and communications devices
  • Desktop applications
  • Mobile applications (iOS/Android)
  • Cloud-based SaaS platforms
  • AWS cloud infrastructure and security environments

Working closely with software development, product management, and customer care teams, you will take ownership across software testing, CI/CD pipelines, release management, cloud infrastructure, and ongoing system reliability. You will thrive in a collaborative environment where teamwork, continuous improvement, and innovation are highly valued, with the opportunity to contribute fresh ideas and help shape the future direction of our products and processes.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and maintain automated test frameworks across multiple platforms
  • Execute both automated and manual testing for software and hardware-integrated systems
  • Build and maintain Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines
  • Manage development, staging, and production deployment environments
  • Drive end-to-end release management and deployment processes
  • Monitor and secure AWS cloud infrastructure environments
  • Implement and improve monitoring, logging, and security practices
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure successful product delivery and software quality

About You:

You are a proactive and solutions-focused professional who enjoys working as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and contributing new ideas. You bring a strong sense of ownership, a collaborative mindset, and a passion for delivering high-quality outcomes in technically complex environments.

You will ideally bring experience across:

  • Automated testing frameworks such as Selenium, Appium, Pytest, JUnit or Robot Framework
  • Azure DevOps and CI/CD pipeline management
  • AWS services including EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, VPC and CloudWatch
  • Python or other scripting/programming languages
  • Git and modern software development practices
  • DevOps, release management, and cloud security principles

Experience within embedded systems, electrical infrastructure, utilities, energy, or industrial technology environments will be highly regarded.

Why Join Siemens?

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a collaborative and forward-thinking R&D team working on technologies that support critical electrical infrastructure globally. You’ll be part of a business experiencing strong momentum in the market, with the opportunity to contribute to innovative products while continuing to grow and develop your career within a globally recognised organisation.

If you are passionate about software quality, automation, DevOps, and cloud technologies - and enjoy working in technically complex environments - we would love to hear from you.

At Siemens, we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to succeed. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and believe diversity drives innovation and better outcomes for our people, customers, and communities.

Fibre Optic Splicer – South East Queensland


RAD Communications Pty Ltd is looking for an experienced Fibre Optic Splicer to join our growing team delivering projects across South East Queensland.

This is an excellent opportunity to work on major telecommunications infrastructure projects with a well-established company that values quality workmanship, safety and professionalism.

About the Role

You will be responsible for working on live carrier networks, completing high-quality fibre optic splicing, testing and fault finding across a range of telecommunications projects. Assisting with the installation of cables in High value MDU and Rail Corridor environments.

Essential Requirements

  • Proven experience splicing on carrier fibre networks

  • Experience working with live fibre networks

  • High standard of fibre optic splicing and enclosure preparation

  • Competent in OTDR and insertion loss testing

  • Ability to interpret fibre schematics and network plans

  • Strong commitment to safety and quality

  • Current Australian Driver Licence

  • Construction White Card

Highly Regarded

  • RIW (Rail Industry Worker) card

  • Experience working within rail corridors

  • Confined Space and Working at Heights tickets

  • First Aid and CPR

  • Telecommunications qualifications

What We Offer

  • Above award wages based on experience

  • Overtime and away work opportunities

  • Company vehicle (for the right applicant)

  • Ongoing training and career development

  • A supportive team environment with varied and interesting projects

  • Long-term career opportunities with a growing telecommunications contractor

If you're an experienced fibre splicer who takes pride in your work and enjoys working on critical telecommunications infrastructure, we'd love to hear from you.

• Full Time | 9-day fortnight | 36 hours per week
  • Salary: From $121,839 to $137,304 per annum plus Superannuation
  • Position Brief - Enterprise Platform Entity Lead

About the role

As the Enterprise Platform Entity Lead, you will work closely with Product Managers, Product Owners, and multiple vendors to track and optimise the flow of value, ensuring that our ERP platform delivers maximum impact. You will lead a dynamic team, guiding them through key functions such as quality assurance, system analysis, application development, change management, upgrades, and continuous improvements.

You will also oversee the management, efficiency, and operation of our Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. This is a pivotal role that combines leadership, technical expertise, and strategic vision to ensure our ERP system is optimally aligned with the City’s operational and strategic goals. The role is responsible for strategic vendor governance and financial oversight of all ERP systems, ensuring cost efficiency and service excellence.

We’re looking for people who have:

  • Hold a Bachelor Degree in Information Technology, or have demonstrable experience in the management and operational delivery of complex business-critical applications
  • Minimum 5+ years technical leadership providing coaching and mentoring of technical and functional teams of various abilities and strengths in medium/large environments
  • Strong experience in managing ERP systems, preferably within a large corporate environment.
  • Proven track record in leading ERP upgrades, and integrations with other systems.
  • In-depth knowledge of ERP platforms (e.g., TechnologyOne, SAP, Oracle), including system architecture, configuration, and customisation.
  • Experience with database management, system integrations (e.g., APIs, middleware), and data migration.
  • Experience working in an agile delivery environment.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts into business language for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Proven experience in stakeholder engagement and change management during system implementations.
  • Experience managing a complex vendor ecosystem and controlled ERP budgets to optimize performance, cost, and delivery outcomes.

Be part of shaping the Gold Coast’s future

The Gold Coast is inspired by lifestyle and driven by opportunity. We are the second largest Council in Australia.

We care about being the most flexible government organisation in Australia. We have a range of benefits to help you create the right balance:

  • 9-day fortnight

  • Flexible work arrangements to suit your lifestyle, including hybrid/work from home options, flexible working hours and locations

  • Personal and professional development courses

  • Access to 350+ fitness facilities through Fitness Passport

  • 4 free confidential counselling sessions a year for you or an immediate family member

  • 1 free nutritionist and financial advice session per year

We pride ourselves on our shared mission of being a high-performing, customer-focused organisation which delivers value-for-money services to the community.

How to apply

To submit your application, please click apply now and complete the application form. You will be required to upload your resume and a cover letter (no more than two pages) outlining how your skills, experience and personal attributes will enable you to be successful in this role. Please note that you may also be asked to provide relevant qualifications, where applicable.

Contact Person Ashley Shannon

Contact Email ashannon@goldcoast.qld.gov.au

Closing Date Sunday 12th July 2026/11:59pm AEDT

We’re committed to creating a diverse workplace and inclusive culture. We take pride in people who align with our high-performance principles. We believe that the diverse makeup of our workforce reflects the community we serve. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and identities, and encourage candidates to discuss any support or adjustments they may need throughout the recruitment process.

IT Service Desk Analyst | Autosports Group – Gold Coast - QLD (National IT Team)

Join a leading automotive retail group supporting dealerships across Australia.

Autosports Group is seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Service Desk Analyst based in Gold Coast - QLD to join our national IT team. This role supports users across our dealership network, providing responsive and effective technical assistance to ensure seamless business operations.

About the Role

As part of a national IT support function, you will be the first point of contact for technical issues across multiple dealership sites. You will manage service requests, troubleshoot incidents remotely, and deliver end-to-end IT support in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Monitor and manage Service Desk tickets and incoming calls, prioritising workload to ensure timely resolution
  • Provide remote troubleshooting using diagnostic tools and structured problem-solving techniques
  • Deliver professional, end-to-end IT support to users across the national dealership network
  • Manage user access, system permissions, and IT equipment provisioning
  • Maintain clear and consistent communication with users and stakeholders
  • Ensure accurate documentation, tracking, and resolution of all support requests

About You

  • Strong customer service skills with experience in IT support or a genuine passion for the industry
  • Ability to manage competing priorities and resolve issues efficiently
  • Solid understanding of Microsoft Windows operating systems
  • Knowledge of networking fundamentals and ITIL principles (or willingness to learn)
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to support both technical and non-technical users
  • Highly organised with strong time management and prioritisation skills
  • Able to work independently while contributing to a national team environment
  • Basic understanding of telecommunications technologies
  • Collaborative, adaptable, and solutions-focused

What We Offer

  • Gold Coast - QLD – based role within a national IT team 
  • Modern dealership and office environments
  • Supportive and respectful workplace culture
  • Career development and progression opportunities across a national group
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
  • Family & Friends Referral Program
  • Company car or car allowance

Why Autosports Group?

Autosports Group is a leading automotive retail group representing premium global brands. Our IT team plays a critical role in enabling dealerships nationwide to operate efficiently. This is a great opportunity to build your career in IT while supporting a high-performing national business.

Apply Now

If you're ready to grow your IT career in a fast-paced, national environment, we’d love to hear from you.

• Design, deliver major network infrastructure projects in an enterprise environment
  • Modern technologies - cloud networking, security, wireless, LAN, WAN and voice
  • Senior engineering role with enterprise scope and strong project ownership

Be Bold. Belong. Be More.

At Southern Cross University, we do things differently, and so can you.

A career with us is more than you might expect. Yes, it’s about the heart, connectedness and community you’d hope for in a regional university. But there’s also so much more. Our game changing Southern Cross Model brings a deeper, focused learning experience and our research is deeply connected to our regions, making a difference on a global scale. This is your chance to help us build something new and meaningful for our students, our communities and your career. To drive change, make a real impact, and thrive in a place as unique as you.

You can be good anywhere. Here, you can be great.

Your opportunity

Southern Cross University is continuing to strengthen its enterprise technology platforms to support innovative teaching, research and student experiences. As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a key role in designing, implementing and improving the University’s enterprise network infrastructure across multiple campuses.

Working within a team of five technical engineers, you will contribute to major infrastructure initiatives while maintaining highly secure, resilient and scalable network services. This role offers the opportunity to work across a modern technology stack including, WAN, LAN, cloud networking, security platforms, wireless infrastructure and voice services, while collaborating closely with internal teams and external vendors.

You will make an impact by:

  • Designing and implementing enterprise network infrastructure and major platform improvements
  • Supporting secure, reliable network services across on-premise, private cloud and Azure environments
  • Leading complex engineering work and providing senior technical guidance within the team
  • Collaborating with vendors and internal stakeholders to deliver scalable, resilient technology solutions

What you will bring

You are an experienced Network or Platform Engineer working within a mid-to-large enterprise environment where you contribute to both day-to-day operations and major infrastructure projects. You enjoy solving complex technical challenges, communicating clearly with stakeholders and delivering reliable, secure network services.

To succeed in this role, you will also need:

  • Undergraduate qualifications in Information Technology, and/or with 4+ years’ experience working in a relevant ICT related field such as network engineering or platform infrastructure
  • Strong experience designing and supporting network switching and WiFi infrastructure within enterprise environments
  • Knowledge of contemporary network security standards across on-premise, private cloud and Azure environments
  • Experience provisioning, administering and troubleshooting enterprise network infrastructure
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively with technical vendors, stakeholders and end-users, with the ability to deliver customer-focused technical support
  • Experience working in a higher education or similarly complex organisation, and exposure to enterprise network design, routing protocols, NGFW, ZTNA or load balancing technologies will be highly regarded.
  • A current NSW Working with Children Check

Why Southern Cross University

SCU is proudly a regional university, young, small and vibrant. That means you’ll find more exposure, opportunity and ownership - to grow, achieve and build a career that means more. And you’ll do it alongside passionate people and leaders who go the extra mile to help you succeed. A career with us is about more than just work. It’s about place, community and the life you want to live. With three main campuses at the Gold Coast, Coffs Harbour and the Northern Rivers region of NSW, we’re integral to all these wonderful communities. Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

Your opportunity is:

  • Full-time, fixed term (23 months)
  • Located at Coffs Harbour, NSW 2450.
  • HEW Level 7. The base salary ranges from $108,361 - $117,660 per annum, plus 17% employer’s contribution to superannuation. The total remuneration package is up to $137,662.

You will enjoy a range of perks designed to support your wellbeing, growth and work-life balance. For more information on our benefits and what it’s like to work at SCU, visit Why work at Southern Cross University?

Be more with Southern Cross University today

Southern Cross University values potential as much as experience. If you do not meet all the listed requirements but are excited about the role and confident you can contribute, we encourage you to apply or reach out for more information.

Name: Vivian Cormier

Email: Jobs@scu.edu.au

Applications close 11.30pm AEDT 13 July 2026.

Eligible applicants must have Australian or New Zealand citizenship, Australian permanent residency or current working rights in Australia for the entire duration of the appointment. Employer visa sponsorship is not available for this position.

Applicants must apply online and attach a CV and cover letter. The cover letter must clearly address each selection criterion in the position description. Information to support you prepare your application is available on our website.   

Southern Cross University celebrates the rich diversity of our regions and international community. Be Bold. Belong. Be More.

At Southern Cross University, we do things differently, and so can you.

A career with us is more than you might expect. Yes, it’s about the heart, connectedness and community you’d hope for in a regional university. But there’s also so much more. Our game changing Southern Cross Model brings a deeper, focused learning experience and our research is deeply connected to our regions, making a difference on a global scale. This is your chance to help us build something new and meaningful for our students, our communities and your career. To drive change, make a real impact, and thrive in a place as unique as you.

You can be good anywhere. Here, you can be great.

Your opportunity

Southern Cross University is on an ambitious journey to redefine how technology empowers learning, research and community impact. We’re building a future-ready technology organisation - modern, secure, adaptable and driven by innovation. Our teams are embracing emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, to deliver smarter, more seamless services and to create meaningful benefits for students, staff and the wider community.

We are seeking a Platform Engineer (Network Security, WAN, LAN & WiFi) to join our Level 3 engineering team, based at our Gold Coast campus. The team consists of five technical engineers, including one Technical Lead, all reporting to the Manager, Infrastructure Platforms, within Technology Platforms.

This role sits at the heart of our enterprise network environment. You will support and enhance large-scale switching and wireless infrastructure across multiple campuses, working across telephony/voice, wireless, security and modern network monitoring technologies. It’s a hands-on engineering role offering both autonomous ownership of tasks and collaborative project work with internal teams and external vendors.

If you are ready to step beyond first-line support and take responsibility for enterprise network engineering in a modern environment, this is your opportunity.

You will make an impact by:

  • Administering, provisioning and troubleshooting enterprise network switching and WiFi infrastructure across a multi-campus environment
  • Supporting secure, reliable and high performing network services aligned to current network security standards
  • Providing Level 3 technical support, resolving escalations and contributing to major incident response and structured change management
  • Collaborating with internal stakeholders, application teams and external vendors to deliver resilient and scalable network solutions
  • Contributing to continuous improvement initiatives, including network optimization, monitoring enhancements and infrastructure modernisation

What you will bring

You are currently working as a Network Engineer in a mid-to-large enterprise environment, balancing day-to-day network administration with project-based improvement work. You are technically capable, service-focused and comfortable operating with autonomy and accountability.

To succeed in this role, you will also need:

  • Strong hands-on experience in network switching and WiFi administration, including provisioning, configuration and troubleshooting
  • Clear understanding of contemporary network security standards and their application within enterprise infrastructure
  • Proven ability to provide customer-focused technical network support while communicating effectively with vendors, stakeholders and end users
  • Experience working within complex, multi-site network environments and managing competing priorities
  • Relevant qualifications in Information Technology and / or industry certifications such as CCNA or CCNP or equivalent demonstrated experience
  • Exposure to enterprise network design, routing protocols and modern security technologies such as Next-Generation Firewalls (NGFW), Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), load balancers and network monitoring platforms will also be viewed favourably.

Join a collaborative team supporting a true enterprise network environment, where you can apply your expertise, broaden your technical exposure and play a key role in delivering secure, modern and resilient infrastructure.

Why Southern Cross University

SCU is proudly a regional university, young, small and vibrant. That means you’ll find more exposure, opportunity and ownership - to grow, achieve and build a career that means more. And you’ll do it alongside passionate people and leaders who go the extra mile to help you succeed. A career with us is about more than just work. It’s about place, community and the life you want to live. With three main campuses at the Gold Coast, Coffs Harbour and the Northern Rivers region of NSW, we’re integral to all these wonderful communities. Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

Your opportunity is:

  • Full-time, fixed term (22 months)
  • Located at Gold Coast, QLD 4225. Relocation Assistance, making it easier to join our regional communities
  • HEW Level 6. The base salary ranges from $95,961 - $105,250 per annum, plus 17% employer’s contribution to superannuation. The total remuneration package is up to $123,142.

You will enjoy a range of perks designed to support your wellbeing, growth and work-life balance. For more information on our benefits and what it’s like to work at SCU, visit Why work at Southern Cross University?

Be more with Southern Cross University today

Southern Cross University values potential as much as experience. If you do not meet all the listed requirements but are excited about the role and confident you can contribute, we encourage you to apply or reach out for more information.

About the Company

Skyline Infotech Services is a growing IT consulting and digital solutions provider specialising in business process optimisation, systems implementation, managed technology services and digital transformation.

We partner with organisations to identify operational challenges and deliver tailored ICT solutions that improve efficiency, governance, productivity and service delivery.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced ICT Business Development Manager to lead business development activities, expand our client portfolio and drive the growth of Skyline Infotech Services’ ICT consulting and digital solutions offerings.

The successful candidate will identify new business opportunities, develop relationships with prospective and existing clients, assess client technology requirements and coordinate the preparation of commercially viable ICT solutions.

This role requires a strong understanding of the ICT services market, consultative selling, account development and the commercial application of technology solutions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement business development strategies for the company’s ICT consulting, systems implementation and digital transformation services.

  • Identify and pursue new business opportunities across target industries and market sectors.

  • Research market conditions, emerging technology trends, competitors and prospective client requirements.

  • Establish and maintain relationships with prospective clients, existing customers, strategic partners and key industry stakeholders.

  • Engage with clients to understand their operational, commercial and technology requirements.

  • Work with technical consultants, solution architects and project teams to develop suitable ICT service offerings and solution proposals.

  • Prepare and present business proposals, capability statements, tenders, quotations and client presentations.

  • Negotiate commercial terms, service arrangements and contractual requirements with clients.

  • Manage the business development pipeline from initial lead generation through to proposal, negotiation and client onboarding.

  • Develop account growth strategies for existing clients, including cross-selling and identifying opportunities for additional ICT services.

  • Establish sales targets, revenue objectives and business development performance indicators.

  • Monitor sales performance, conversion rates, market activity and business development outcomes.

  • Maintain accurate records of client interactions, opportunities, proposals and sales activities using CRM and reporting systems.

  • Represent Skyline Infotech Services at client meetings, networking events, industry forums and business development activities.

  • Collaborate with management to support pricing strategies, service development and broader organisational growth objectives.

  • Provide regular reports and recommendations to senior management regarding market opportunities, client feedback and business development performance.

Skills and Experience

Essential

  • Demonstrated experience in ICT business development, technology sales, account management or commercial development.

  • Strong understanding of ICT consulting, digital solutions, systems implementation, managed services or technology transformation projects.

  • Proven ability to identify business opportunities and develop new client relationships.

  • Experience managing the complete business development lifecycle, including lead generation, client engagement, proposal preparation, negotiation and conversion.

  • Strong consultative selling and solution-development capability.

  • Demonstrated experience preparing proposals, tenders, quotations, presentations and commercial documentation.

  • Excellent stakeholder-management, communication, presentation and negotiation skills.

  • Strong commercial awareness and ability to understand client operational and technology requirements.

  • Ability to collaborate effectively with technical specialists, consultants, project managers and senior management.

  • Proficiency in CRM platforms, Microsoft Office, reporting systems and business-development tools.

  • Strong analytical, organisational and problem-solving skills.

Desirable

  • Experience in ICT consulting, ERP systems, cloud services, workflow automation, cybersecurity, managed services or digital transformation.

  • Existing professional networks within relevant commercial or government sectors.

  • Experience responding to formal tenders, requests for proposals and procurement opportunities.

  • Knowledge of government or corporate procurement processes.

  • Familiarity with Agile, hybrid or technology project-delivery methodologies.

  • Relevant qualifications in information technology, business, marketing, commerce or a related discipline.

Benefits

  • Opportunity to lead business development activities within a growing ICT consulting and digital solutions company.

  • Exposure to a diverse range of ICT, automation and digital transformation projects.

Visa sponsorship may be considered for a suitably qualified and experienced candidate who meets all eligibility requirements.

IT and Technical Support Specialist - Entry Level

Part Time | $60K - $70K + Super Pro Rata | Southport

For more than 30 years, InBody has been a global leader in body composition analysis technology, helping people better understand their health through accurate and innovative data. Operating in over 110 countries with more than 1,000 employees worldwide, InBody's technology is trusted by healthcare providers, hospitals, fitness centres, universities, professional sporting organisations, and corporate wellness programs.

As the company expands, InBody Oceania is looking for a motivated and proactive IT and Technical Support Specialist to join their growing team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate, current student, junior IT professional, or service technician looking to develop their career in a supportive and dynamic environment.

This role combines customer service, technical support, field service, and software assistance. Full product and systems training will be provided, making it ideal for someone with a growth mindset and willingness to learn.

About the Role

You will be the first point of contact for customers requiring technical assistance and support. Your responsibilities will include a mix of remote customer support and onsite customer visits across QLD.

Key Responsibilities

  • Respond to customer enquiries via phone, email, and support tickets.

  • Provide software and technical support for InBody products and applications.

  • Assist customers with software integrations and troubleshooting.

  • Liaise with international support teams to resolve technical issues.

  • Support customer onboarding and training.

  • Conduct onsite service visits, maintenance, and device health checks.

  • Assist with installations, commissioning, and equipment setup.

  • Complete service reports and documentation.

  • Build strong relationships with customers and proactively identify service opportunities.

About You

We're open to candidates from a variety of backgrounds, including:

  • IT Support

  • Help Desk / Service Desk

  • Technical Customer Service

  • Service Technician

  • Electronics or Hardware Support

  • Recent Graduates

  • Current Students

To Be Successful, You Will Have:

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills.

  • A proactive and solutions-focused attitude.

  • Basic IT troubleshooting skills.

  • Confidence speaking with customers in person, over the phone, and via email.

  • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

  • Ability to work independently and manage priorities.

  • A willingness to learn new systems and technologies.

Highly Regarded

  • Experience in technical support or customer service.

  • Experience working with software applications.

  • Experience with device installations or servicing.

What We Offer

  • Comprehensive training and ongoing support.

  • Exposure to both technical support and field service.

  • Career development and progression pathways.

  • Opportunity to progress into a full-time position.

  • Supportive and collaborative team environment.

  • Diverse and engaging role with no two days the same.

Hours & Location

  • Southport based office.

  • Initially part-time (approximately 24 hours per week across 3 days).

  • Opportunity to transition to full-time as the business continues to grow.

  • Some travel required.


If you're looking for an opportunity to build a career in customer support, technical services, and IT while working with innovative health technology, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now to join the team at InBody Oceania.

Marymount Primary is a co-educational Catholic primary school which caters for over 1000 students and is located on the southern end of the Gold Coast. We are a predominantly 6-stream school which has an inclusive education policy and is situated on the same campus as Marymount College.

About Worklocker

Worklocker is a rapidly growing Australian workwear and safety franchise, built on strong operational foundations and genuine partnerships with our franchise network. We're in the middle of a significant technology transformation - rolling out Odoo as our ERP across our national store network - and we're investing in the delivery leadership to make sure it lands well, not just on paper.

The Opportunity

We're looking for someone to support the end-to-end delivery of our ERP program - from getting stores live, to keeping the development team moving, to making sure what goes into production actually works.

This is a hands-on delivery role with real accountability. You'll sit at the centre of a cross-functional team spanning development, QA, operations, and franchise partners. You'll own the requirements before dev starts, the quality before go-live, and the outcomes after. You'll be working across a growing network of retail stores nationally, coordinating delivery across a small, focused team.

You'll also be joining a team that's genuinely open to using AI tooling to work smarter. If you've got ideas about how to get more done with less overhead, we want to hear them.

What You'll Be Doing

Store Onboarding & Delivery

  • Own the technical end-to-end implementation of new stores onto the ERP platform from scoping through to go-live and stabilisation

  • Stay connected post-launch to ensure stores are genuinely embedded before handover to BAU

  • Translate operational complexity into structured, executable delivery plans

Requirements & Development

  • Define and sign off complete, unambiguous requirements before development begins

  • Work directly with developers to ensure briefs are clear, questions are answered fast, and rework is the exception, not the norm

  • Maintain a prioritised, well-managed backlog that reflects real business priorities and push back when everything can't be high priority

QA & Release

  • Lead the quality assessment that drives the go/no-go conversation

  • Manage environments across dev, staging and production, and ensure releases are controlled and documented

  • Leverage QA support to scale coverage without sacrificing quality

Stakeholder Management

  • Keep the wider team informed proactively, not reactively

  • Surface resourcing issues, dependency risks and scope changes early, with a recommended path forward, not just a flag

  • Be the person stakeholders trust to tell them the truth about where things are at

Delivery Coordination

  • Keep the team organised and moving with sprint rhythms, prioritisation, blockers resolved

  • Find smarter ways to coordinate and deliver visibility including AI tools where they genuinely help

About You

You're a delivery person, not necessarily a document person. You get energy from seeing things ship. You're comfortable holding developers to a standard, managing up when something needs escalating, and making a call when quality isn't where it needs to be.

This is a great step up for someone who's been hands-on with Odoo in a retail environment and is ready to coordinate delivery end-to-end.

  • Hands-on experience with Odoo is essential - retail or franchise context is a strong advantage

  • Strong understanding of ERP concepts including inventory, POS, product catalogue, fulfilment, integrations

The City of Gold Coast is one of Australia's largest and most dynamic local governments, delivering services to a community of more than 650,000 people across one of the nation's fastest-growing cities. About the team

The team works across organisational boundaries — partnering with data owners, ICT, legal, privacy officers, and executive leadership — to embed governance where the data actually lives.

Two specialist roles (Senior Data Governance Officer and Information & Data Uplift Lead) are commencing together in July 2026, giving you a rare opportunity to help shape the function from the ground up alongside a peer hire, supported by an experienced manager and an established platform capability.

About the role

As Senior Data Governance Officer, you will lead the practical design and implementation of the City's Data Governance Framework — moving from policy to practice, and from aspiration to measurable outcomes. This is a role about influence, change, and enabling people to work with data confidently and responsibly.

You will:

• Design and embed data governance policies, standards, and procedures that are fit-for-purpose across a complex, multi-directorate organisation.

• Establish and facilitate a Data Governance Council and data stewardship network, building the human architecture of good governance.

• Lead metadata management, data classification, data quality frameworks, and stewardship practices in alignment with the City's Enterprise Data Platform (Microsoft Fabric/ Purview/ OneLake)

• Ensure compliance with Queensland legislative obligations including the Public Records Act 2023, Information Privacy Act 2009, and relevant QSA Standards.

• Promote privacy-by-design and ethical data use principles, translating regulatory obligations into practical team-level guidance.

• Advise on AI-readiness governance — helping the organisation unlock the value of its data assets safely and responsibly.

• Produce executive-level reporting, governance dashboards, and progress metrics for senior leadership and committee audiences.

• Drive data literacy and governance culture uplift across the organisation through targeted engagement, training, and communications.

This is a hybrid role. You will work primarily from our Bundall Precinct offices, with flexibility to work from home on non-collaborative days. We trust our people to manage their time professionally.

What Success Looks Like in This Role

• Standing up a functioning data stewardship network with active data custodians and stewards.

• Visible executive confidence in data governance maturity and risk posture.

• A team culture where governance is seen as an enabler — not a blocker.

We’re looking for people who have:

• Ability to engage, influence, and build trust with senior stakeholders, data custodians, and cross-functional teams — without line authority.

Highly Regarded

Why Southern Cross University

Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

  • Located at either Coffs Harbour, NSW 2450, Gold Coast, QLD 4225, or Lismore, NSW 2480. Relocation Assistance, making it easier to join our regional communities

jobs.job_description:
Level 2 IT Support Engineer – Healthcare IT

Upper Coomera, Gold Coast, QLD
Casual\Full Time | Healthcare Managed Services Provider (MSP)

At Focus I.T. Healthcare Solutions, we specialise in providing IT support and technology solutions to medical practices and healthcare providers across Queensland.

Our philosophy is simple: quick resolution, exceptional service, and long-term client relationships.

We pride ourselves on doing the extra 10% that others don't. It's this commitment to excellence that has helped us become a trusted technology partner for healthcare providers and a workplace where talented IT professionals can build rewarding careers.

We're looking for a motivated and customer-focused Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join our growing team.

If you enjoy solving problems, working with a wide variety of technologies, and want to learn from an experienced team of healthcare IT specialists, we'd love to hear from you.

About the Role

This role combines remote support, onsite support, project work, and customer relationship management.

You will be responsible for supporting medical practices and healthcare providers with a wide range of technologies while delivering exceptional customer service.

This is not a role for someone seeking a strictly 9-to-5 environment. We take ownership of our responsibilities and work together to ensure our clients receive the support they need when they need it.

In return, you'll gain exposure to complex IT environments, healthcare technologies, cloud platforms, networking, cybersecurity, and infrastructure projects.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide Level 1 and Level 2 technical support to clients.

  • Troubleshoot desktop, server, networking, and cloud-based issues.

  • Manage support tickets through to resolution.

  • Support Microsoft 365 environments.

  • Configure and deploy desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and printers.

  • Join workstations to domains and configure user environments.

  • Monitor and respond to RMM alerts and notifications.

  • Assist with backup monitoring and verification.

  • Support VPN and remote access solutions.

  • Escalate complex issues where required.

  • Create and maintain technical documentation.

  • Deliver onsite and remote support to clients.

  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and internal projects.

Essential Skills & Experience

  • Previous experience working in an MSP or IT support environment.

  • Strong Windows 11 troubleshooting skills.

  • Experience with Microsoft 365 administration.

  • Understanding of Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

  • Proven ability to manage a ticket queue effectively.

  • Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving abilities.

  • Ability to prioritise and work under pressure.

  • Current Australian Driver's Licence.

Desirable Skills

Experience with any of the following will be highly regarded:

  • Best Practice or Medical Director software.

  • Windows Server 2022–2025

  • Hyper-V and VMware vSphere.

  • Microsoft Exchange and SharePoint.

  • Mobile Device Management (MDM).

  • Apple macOS support.

  • Datto, Kaseya, and RMM platforms.

  • Cybersecurity solutions and endpoint protection.

  • Microsoft Certifications.

  • Certificate III or IV in Information Technology.

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary package.

  • Ongoing training and professional development.

  • Exposure to modern technologies and healthcare IT environments.

  • Career progression opportunities.

  • Supportive team culture.

  • Company uniform provided.

  • Opportunity to work with one of Queensland's leading healthcare-focused MSPs.

Mandatory Requirements

Due to the healthcare environments we support:

  • Successful applicants must have the right to work in Australia.

  • National Police Check may be required.

How to Apply

To be considered, applicants must submit:

  1. A current resume.

  2. A cover letter outlining:

    • Why are you suitable for the role?

    • Your MSP experience (if applicable).

    • Why would you like to join Focus I.T. Healthcare Solutions?

Applications without a cover letter will not be considered.

If you're ready to build your IT career with a fast-growing healthcare technology company, we'd love to hear from you.

jobs.job_description:
Are you a passionate & caring person looking to develop your training skills in a positive environment?

We are looking for enthusiastic and people-focused individuals that love to share their knowledge and skills with others. Candidates will have great communication skills with a diverse scope of people and willingness to learn and serve students towards a greater understanding. This role is remote and is suited for people all across Australia.


Application Information:

To apply for this role, you must:

  1. Answer the Video Submission Question here - https://vocalvideo.com/c/eqc-institute-trainer

  2. Provide a resume highlighting your experience, knowledge, and skills relevant to this role. (Submit this via Seek)



Job Role:
Provide education and training to students via Online Course Coaching.

Being a Course Coach involves:

  • Tutoring and teaching adult learners. This is done by having consistent communication with students via Zoom calls, phone or written communication.

  • General support and connection with students about course questions and study requirements.

  • Basic scheduling and time management skills are required for a successful candidate.

  • Working with a passionate team to ensure student success.


We are currently hiring course coaches with Cyber Security skills and experience or Full Stack Web Development skills. If you meet either one of these skills please apply below!

Skills Needed Cyber:

  • Experience with Cyber Security Systems and Tools (Basic Cloudflare, AWS, Wireshark, Virtual Machines)

  • Strong understanding of Cyber Security foundations

  • Industry experience is preferred


Skills Needed Web Development:

  • Experience in Full Stack Web Development

  • Proficiency with at least 2 programming languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python)

  • Experience with Github and SQL or similar database programing

  • Experience with Blockchain and AI / ML is preferred


Universal Training Skills:

  • Strong English Communication Skills

  • Computer with appropriate microphone and camera settings to host a zoom call

  • Flexible availability to communicate with students

  • Valid TAE (training and assessment) qualification - if you do not have this, be willing to obtain the qualification.
     

Salary:

This is a part-time role. The part-time rate for a Course Coach for this particular role is $45 per hour. Successful candidates will be doing 10 hours per week.

This job is dependent on student availability and self-autonomy for scheduling. Hours are averaged across the week, with hours being dependent on student availability.


About Us

We are a Vocational Education and Training Organisation who's team has been operating for over 10 years in this space. We make sure to always focus on delivering quality materials, practical training and always aim to find new ways that can raise the bar across different industries. What sets us apart is our commitment to and incredible student experience whilst studying.


We work together; listening and challenging to create a supportive work environment whereby everyone works together and is equal.


We are hiring ASAP so if you think this role suits you, we would love to get in touch with you.


Work with us, to work together, and as a team let's inspire the next round of industry leaders!

• Full Time Fixed Term (until June 2027) and Full Times Roles Available | 9-day fortnight | 36 hours per week

  • Position Brief - Senior Network Engineer

About the team

We're entering a new chapter for the Chief Technology Office at the City of Gold Coast as we deliver bold, future-focused solutions. With the launch of our IT Operating Model and the transition of critical IT services in-house, we are transforming the way we deliver technology—making it faster, more responsive, and more aligned with the needs of our organisation and customers.

As part of this transformation, we're growing our network engineering capability and have two exciting Senior Network Engineer opportunities available. Whether you're passionate about delivering strategic technology uplift projects or supporting and enhancing enterprise network operations, there's an opportunity to make a significant impact.

About the role

Senior Network Engineer – Network Management Uplift Project

This role is a key contributor to the City's Network Management Uplift Project, focusing on the design and delivery of secure, resilient, and modern network services.

Working closely with network, infrastructure, security, and project teams, you will help deliver initiatives including network access control, identity-based access, Zero Trust architecture, network segmentation, and security uplift activities. While primarily project-focused, you will also contribute to business-as-usual network operations and continuous improvement initiatives.

Senior Network Engineer – Network Operations & Infrastructure

This role is responsible for delivering complex network infrastructure projects and providing ongoing support for the City's enterprise network environment.

You will take ownership of network design, implementation, commissioning, optimisation, and operational support across a diverse range of City facilities. This hands-on role combines project delivery with operational excellence and offers the opportunity to work across a broad range of technologies and environments.

Participation in an on-call roster is required for both positions.

What you'll be doing

  • Designing, implementing, and supporting enterprise network solutions
  • Managing routing, switching, firewall, and network security technologies
  • Delivering network infrastructure projects from design through to implementation
  • Monitoring, troubleshooting, and optimising network performance
  • Supporting cloud networking and hybrid connectivity solutions
  • Collaborating with stakeholders across technology and business teams
  • Producing technical documentation, designs, and diagrams
  • Contributing to continuous improvement and operational excellence
  • Participating in after-hours support and incident response when required

What we're looking for

  • Extensive experience in enterprise network engineering within large and complex environments
  • Strong understanding of network architecture, security principles, and best practices
  • Expert proficiency in routing protocols including OSPF and BGP
  • Experience with enterprise firewall technologies, including Cisco and Check Point
  • Experience with Cisco DNA Centre (DNAC) and Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE)
  • Cloud networking experience, particularly within Microsoft Azure environments
  • Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
  • Experience producing technical designs and documentation using tools such as Microsoft Visio
  • A valid driver's licence and willingness to travel throughout the Gold Coast region

Highly desirable for the Network Management Uplift Project role

  • Experience implementing and supporting Network Access Control (NAC) solutions
  • Strong knowledge of IEEE 802.1X authentication
  • Cisco ISE policy design, profiling, posture assessment, and TrustSec
  • Experience integrating Microsoft Intune with network access controls
  • Applied experience with Zero Trust networking principles
  • Experience with Active Directory, PKI/certificate services, and identity-based access controls

Qualifications

  • CCNA, JNCIA, or equivalent industry certification (desirable)

Be part of shaping the Gold Coast’s future

The Gold Coast is inspired by lifestyle and driven by opportunity. We are the second largest Council in Australia.

We care about being the most flexible government organisation in Australia. We have a range of benefits to help you create the right balance:

  • 9-day fortnight
  • Flexible work arrangements to suit your lifestyle, including hybrid/work from home options, flexible working hours and locations
  • Personal and professional development courses
  • Access to 350+ fitness facilities through Fitness Passport
  • 4 free confidential counselling sessions a year for you or an immediate family member
  • 1 free nutritionist and financial advice session per year

We pride ourselves on our shared mission of being a high-performing, customer-focused organisation which delivers value-for-money services to the community.

How to apply

To submit your application, please click apply now and complete the application form. You will be required to upload your resume and a cover letter (no more than two pages) outlining how your skills, experience and personal attributes will enable you to be successful in this role. Please note that you may also be asked to provide relevant qualifications, where applicable.

Contact Person Ashley Shannon
Contact Email ashannon@goldcoast.qld.gov.au
Closing Date Thursday, 02 July 2026 at 11:59pm AEDT

Our selection process may include a one-way video interview via Criteria, a face-to-face or virtual interview via Microsoft Teams, reference checks via Xref and additional checks including police checks, employment history checks, qualification checks, pre-employment medical reviews and drug and alcohol testing.

We’re committed to creating a diverse workplace and inclusive culture. We take pride in people who align with our high-performance principles. We believe that the diverse makeup of our workforce reflects the community we serve. We consider all qualified candidates for employment, irrespective of race, age, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or other protected characteristics by law.

Founded in 1927, KENT WORLDWIDE is a family-owned US company built around specialty ingredients and consumer brands. Over the years, the business has become known around the world for our strength in manufacturing and marketing.

At Kent Consumer Brands APAC, our ambition is to create joyful memories every day, for everybody, around the world. For nearly a century, our core values of Honesty, Quality, Safety, Timeliness, Win Win Partnerships and Curiosity have shaped our culture and long-term success.

We are a fast growing, values driven organisation with a strong portfolio of trusted brands including Frosty Boy, Sqwincher, Breeder’s Choice and World’s Best Cat Litter. With multiple manufacturing sites across Australia and the UK and products sold in more than 70 countries, we are proud to employ hundreds of people who are passionate about quality, sustainability and innovation.

Joining us means being part of a supportive, growth-oriented business that invests in its people, its brands and its future.

About the Role

We’re looking for a proactive and customer-focused Service Desk Technician II to join our IT team supporting multiple manufacturing and head office sites across Australia.

This is a hands-on, visible role where you’ll be the local face of IT, partnering with site operational leaders, end users, vendors, and global technology teams to ensure technology runs smoothly and effectively.

You’ll provide Level 1 & 2 support across a broad tech landscape including Microsoft 365, endpoints, applications, and infrastructure, while also contributing to projects, system improvements, and technology initiatives.

This role will be based at our Helensvale and Yatala locations, with occasional interstate travel to our manufacturing sites. 

What You’ll Do

  • Provide professional, friendly and timely Level 1 & 2 IT support across hardware, software, and systems
  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues across Microsoft 365, endpoints, applications, and networks
  • Log, manage, and resolve incidents within agreed service delivery timeframes
  • Install, configure, and maintain desktops, laptops, tablets, and peripherals
  • Support onboarding, user setup, and executive-level support
  • Maintain accurate IT documentation, asset registers, and inventory tracking
  • Deliver end-user training and guidance to improve technology adoption
  • Identify and implement process improvements within the service desk
  • Support IT projects, system upgrades, and infrastructure initiatives

What You’ll Bring

  • Degree in Computer Science or equivalent (preferred)
  • 4+ years’ experience in Service Desk / IT Support roles
  • 2+ years’ experience supporting ICT environments in complex organisations
  • Strong troubleshooting and incident management skills
  • Hands-on experience with Microsoft technologies, networking, and infrastructure
  • Understanding of ITIL and service delivery frameworks
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
  • Ability to prioritise, multitask, and work independently
  • Ability to travel regularly between our Helensvale and Yatala locations, as well as occasional interstate travel to our manufacturing sites

What’s In It For You

  • Exposure to both manufacturing and corporate IT environments
  • Opportunity to work with global technology teams
  • Involvement in projects, upgrades, and transformation initiatives
  • Supportive and collaborative team culture

About the role

We are seeking an individual with solid IT knowledge with daytime availability to join our small team at GC Tech Services as an in-house IT support technician. This role involves both on-site work in clients’ homes, businesses and remote support.

This is a highly flexible, casual role that will vary based on your availability and client demand.

Typical hours are 2–5 hours per week, with busier weeks reaching 10+ hours. Typically bookings are made within 24 hours of the client contacting us.

What you'll be doing

  • Responding to and resolving IT related queries and issues in a timely manner

  • IT Service Desk

  • Outstanding customer service and communication skills

  • Enjoys problem soliving and managing multiple things at once

 

This opportunity would suit candidates seeking flexible employment within a professional IT environment, whether alongside their studies or other part-time commitments, while gaining practical experience in a supportive and outcomes-focused team.

The hourly rate for these casual roles starts from $47.05 (includes 25% casual loading) plus 12% superannuation.


Further information

If you're someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is eager to grow and make your mark, this is the opportunity you've been waiting for. We want to hear from you directly - apply now with your CV and a letter telling us why you're the perfect fit for this role.
