You know how to sell complex services into SMB and mid-market organisations.

Not products. Not licences. Not simple transactional deals. Services.

First Focus is looking for a proven Senior Solution Consultant who can turn qualified conversations into high-quality commercial outcomes. We are the leading managed AI and IT services provider in Australia and New Zealand, and our marketing engine creates a strong flow of opportunities with businesses already exploring change.

Your role is to qualify well, run sharp discovery, shape the right solution and close clients who are a strong fit for our managed AI, managed IT, cloud, cyber, modern workplace and project capabilities.

This is a senior individual contributor role. It is not a training-ground sales position, and it is not a lead generation role. You will need to arrive with mature discovery skills, commercial discipline and the ability to build trust with executives quickly.

What You Will Be Doing

You will work with businesses typically in the 20 to 200 seat range that are reviewing their IT environment, AI initiatives, security posture, systems maturity or current technology partner.

AI is at the forefront of this role. First Focus is the market leader in helping SMBs do more with AI in practical, secure and commercially useful ways. You will be at the heart of those conversations, helping business leaders move from curiosity and experimentation to clear priorities, sensible adoption and measurable outcomes.

You will lead the commercial conversation from qualification through to proposal and close. That means understanding the client’s business, identifying real pain, testing urgency, mapping decision process and shaping a solution that is commercially sound and operationally deliverable.

You will work closely with technical specialists, but you will own the sales process. You do not need to be the deepest technical person in the room. You do need to know how to translate AI, security, cloud and managed services capability into business value, ask strong questions and keep the conversation moving toward a decision.

You will write clear, credible proposals that help senior stakeholders understand the case for change. You will also ensure clean handovers into onboarding and delivery, because selling something properly includes setting the client and the delivery team up for success.

About You

You have already sold complex B2B services and you understand that services sales is different from product sales. Trust matters earlier. Discovery matters more. The value has to be made clear before the client can confidently move.

You are curious about AI and already using it to work smarter. You experiment with it in your professional and personal life, not because it is trendy, but because you can see how it improves research, communication, productivity, decision-making and client outcomes.

You are likely to be a strong fit if you have:

  • At least five years of consultative selling, solution consulting or services sales experience.

  • A track record selling managed services, professional services, IT consulting, cyber, cloud, systems integration, AI-enabled services or similar complex services.

  • Experience selling into SMB or mid-market organisations.

  • Confidence speaking with owners, CEOs, CFOs, COOs, IT leaders and senior managers.

  • The ability to qualify firmly and avoid wasting time on weak opportunities.

  • Strong written communication and the ability to produce clear proposals.

  • Enough technical curiosity to learn our service lines quickly and know when to bring in specialists.

  • A genuine interest in AI and how it can help businesses improve performance.

  • Commercial maturity, high standards and the expectation that your remuneration should reflect the value you create.

This role is probably not right if your experience is mainly lead generation, account management, product sales or enterprise sales with large internal bid teams around you. It is also not right if you need a highly scripted sales process to be effective, or if AI is something you only talk about rather than actively use.

What Great Looks Like

Within six months, you will be running strong executive discovery with limited supervision, converting a meaningful percentage of assigned opportunities, producing commercially sharp proposals and contributing materially to new recurring revenue.

You will be trusted by prospects because you understand their business. You will be trusted internally because you qualify properly, sell accurately and set expectations clearly.

You will also be helping SMB leaders understand where AI can create real business value, where they need better foundations first and how First Focus can help them move forward safely and practically.

Why First Focus

First Focus is the leading managed AI and IT services provider in Australia and New Zealand, with more than 350 specialists across Australia, New Zealand and the Philippines.

We are at the front of helping SMBs use AI to work smarter, improve productivity, strengthen decision-making and modernise the way they operate. You will have genuine delivery depth behind you, strong brand credibility and a proven marketing engine creating qualified demand.

You will not be building a pipeline from scratch. You will be working with businesses that already know who we are and are actively exploring options. Your job is to turn the right opportunities into long-term client relationships.

Benefits

  • Strong base-weighted remuneration with achievable performance incentives.

  • Hybrid work and flexible hours.

  • Paid study days and structured professional development.

  • Generous parental leave and wellbeing programs.

  • Extra leave for volunteering or community work.

  • Referral bonuses and profit share, subject to eligibility.

  • Access to deep technical capability across managed AI, managed IT, cloud, cyber, modern workplace and projects.

If you already know how to sell professional services, are genuinely curious about AI and want a senior IC role with qualified demand, strong delivery capability and serious earning potential, we would be happy to speak with you.

Questions before applying? Reach out to Brendan Ritchie for a confidential conversation.

First Focus welcomes applications from Neurodiverse candidates. We recommend & appreciate disclosure along with the application so that a copy of our Neurodiversity Statement can be provided outlining the flexible options we can offer to best showcase your skills.

Be Bold. Belong. Be More.

At Southern Cross University, we do things differently, and so can you.

A career with us is more than you might expect. Yes, it’s about the heart, connectedness and community you’d hope for in a regional university. But there’s also so much more. Our game changing Southern Cross Model brings a deeper, focused learning experience and our research is deeply connected to our regions, making a difference on a global scale. This is your chance to help us build something new and meaningful for our students, our communities and your career. To drive change, make a real impact, and thrive in a place as unique as you.

You can be good anywhere. Here, you can be great.

Your opportunity

Southern Cross University is on an ambitious journey to redefine how technology empowers learning, research, and community impact. We’re building a future-ready technology organisation – modern, secure, adaptable, and driven by innovation.  Our teams are embracing emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, to deliver smarter services and real-world benefits for students, staff, and the wider community.

Within Technology Services, our five dynamic work units – Data, Architecture & Integration, Cyber Resilience, Digital Delivery & Innovation, Technology Platforms, and Operations & Services – work collaboratively to deliver high-impact solutions across the University.

We are seeking a Solution Architect to lead the design and delivery of complex, enterprise-wide solutions that span multiple systems, platforms, and business domains.  This senior technical role offers the chance to shape architectural direction, establish design standards, and drive strategic solution initiatives across the University’s teaching, learning, research, and administrative functions.

Reporting to the Enterprise Architect, you will work closely with development teams, project managers, and business stakeholders to deliver high-impact solutions aligned with the University’s enterprise architecture vision

You will make an impact by:

  • Designing and delivering robust enterprise-grade solution architectures that support teaching, research, student services, and operations across all major university domains
  • Leading the integration of core platforms such as TechnologyOne, Microsoft Dynamics, Blackboard, Microsoft Teams, and custom applications, ensuring seamless connectivity across all systems
  • Establishing and promoting architectural standards and frameworks that ensure scalability, security, and maintainability across all digital initiatives
  • Providing expert guidance and mentorship to develop teams, driving best practices in solution design, technology selection, and implementation
  • Collaborating with stakeholders and contributing to the university technology roadmap, championing innovation, emerging technologies, and long term strategic planning

What you will bring

You’re an experienced and forward-thinking Solution Architect with deep technical knowledge, strong leadership skills, and a collaborative approach to problem solving.  You thrive in complex environments, enjoy working across multiple business domains, and are passionate about delivering scalable, impactful technology solutions. 

To succeed in this role, you will also need:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field
  • Ideally 8+ years in technical roles with at least 5 years in solution architecture
  • Proven experience in designing and integrating enterprise platforms including TechnologyOne, Dynamics 365, Blackboard, and Microsoft Teams
  • Strong understanding of solution design patterns, architectural frameworks, and integration practices
  • Demonstrate ability to lead large scale architecture initiatives and collaborate with cross-functional teams
  • Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, translating business needs into technical strategy
  • A commitment to innovation, continuous improvement, and delivering real world impact 

Why Southern Cross University

SCU is proudly a regional university, young, small and vibrant. That means you’ll find more exposure, opportunity and ownership - to grow, achieve and build a career that means more. And you’ll do it alongside passionate people and leaders who go the extra mile to help you succeed. A career with us is about more than just work. It’s about place, community and the life you want to live. With three main campuses at the Gold Coast, Coffs Harbour and the Northern Rivers region of NSW, we’re integral to all these wonderful communities. Find your place and be more in this beautiful part of the world.

Your opportunity is:

  • Full-time, continuing (permanent)
  • Located at either Coffs Harbour, NSW 2450, Gold Coast, QLD 4225, or Lismore, NSW 2480. Relocation Assistance, making it easier to join our regional communities
  • The level of appointment will be commensurate with the candidate’s qualifications, skills and experience.

You will enjoy a range of perks designed to support your wellbeing, growth and work-life balance. For more information on our benefits and what it’s like to work at SCU, visit Why work at Southern Cross University?

Be more with Southern Cross University today

Southern Cross University values potential as much as experience. If you do not meet all the listed requirements but are excited about the role and confident you can contribute, we encourage you to apply or reach out for more information.

Job Description

Frank ICT Pty Ltd, trading as Phone Central, is a telecommunications and business communications provider specialising in the installation, maintenance and support of enterprise communication systems across Australia. Due to continued growth, we are seeking a skilled Telecommunications Field Engineer to join our technical team on a full-time basis.

This position requires the application of specialised technical knowledge to design, install, commission, monitor, troubleshoot and support telecommunications networks and systems.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will be responsible for:

  • Design and document business telecommunications solutions, including VoIP, SIP, cloud/on-premise PBX, unified communications, contact centre, internet/NBN, mobile data and LAN/WAN connectivity required to support telecommunications voice and data performance.

  • Assess client sites and existing telecommunications infrastructure, including carrier services, call flows, network topology, IP addressing, routers, switches, Wi-Fi, handsets, cabling pathways and associated telecommunications-dependent systems and equipment.

  • Prepare and coordinate technical implementation, migration and cutover plans, equipment schedules, network diagrams, configuration records and technical documentation.

  • Commission, configure, test, maintain and repair telecommunications platforms and equipment, including iPECS, SIP trunks, VoIP handsets, call routing, IVR, voicemail, conferencing, call recording, contact centre functions and supporting network devices.

  • Monitor, troubleshoot and optimise telecommunications system performance, including call quality, latency, jitter, packet loss, bandwidth, trunk capacity, failover, service availability, extension usage and contact centre metrics.

  • Diagnose and resolve complex telecommunications and associated network faults, including SIP, routing, firewall/NAT, VLAN/QoS, carrier/NBN, provisioning, call quality and congestion issues.

  • Provide specialist technical advice to clients and internal teams, and coordinate with carriers, ISPs, NBN channels, vendors and technology partners on telecommunications design, migration, activations, number porting, escalations, firmware updates, risks and upgrade requirements.

  • Integrate telecommunications systems with supporting business infrastructure, maintain technical records, complete handover and administrator training, support ongoing maintenance, and ensure compliance with technical, manufacturer, WHS and licensing requirements.

Minimum Requirements

To be considered for this role, applicants must demonstrate:

  • A Diploma or higher qualification in a telecommunications or related field.

  • 1 year full-time experience in a telecommunications or related technical role. Strong candidates with 5+ years of relevant experience will be highly regarded.

  • Practical experience with VoIP, SIP, PBX, cloud telephony, on-premise phone systems, business internet/NBN services, routers, switches, VLANs, QoS, wireless networking and IP-based endpoints.

jobs.job_title: Software Developer Hybrid Cloud Semantic Layer
jobs.job_description:
The role

Safiery delivers systems to vehicle and vessel OEM’s in the auxiliary power, digital switching, video capture and 360 viewing space. We have 3D displays and use a wide range of IOT sensors in the marine and vehicle domains.

We have a development team of 11 people and build our own open source controllers, equipped with NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX16gb modules on our controller boards.

This role is in the layer above the controller and display development. Its “hybrid cloud” with open framework deployment. You would build and harden the single in-vehicle / in-vessel open semantic tree (COVESA VSS + Eclipse Kuksa on the chassis, Signal K on the hull) so that a digital twin is achieved from the physical vehicle or vessel.

What you'll work on

•          The fabric. A Kuksa databroker (Rust, VSS) on the vehicle and a Signal K server (Node.js) on the boat, each holding current + intended state. Actuators are also sensors: consumers write a target (or PUT a path), the owning provider actuates and publishes the achieved value back.

•          The provider layer. Lightweight processes that bridge one bus or device family to VSS leaves / Signal K paths — applying deadband and rate throttling, holding persistent warm sessions, and enforcing single-writer ownership so no two components write the same value.

•          Buses, normalised once at the edge. CAN (VE.Can, STAR), Victron / Venus OS dbus, OBD-II/J1939, RV-C, LIN, Modbus RTU/RS485, Matter/Thread, BLE BMS — and, on the marine side, the licensed legacy world (NMEA 2000 / 0183 / Seatalk1) parsed behind a gateway so consumers never see a raw PGN.

•          Identity and twin. Our QDID device-identity framework keys both the edge tree and the digital twin; you'll help keep the QDID-keyed, path-keyed structure byte-for-byte identical edge and cloud, over MQTT + WireGuard.

Responsibilities

•          Design, build and test providers/PUT-handlers in Rust and/or Node.js (with C/C++ for firmware-adjacent bus work).

•          Maintain and govern the Safiery VSS and Signal K overlays as controlled, core-compatible artifacts.

•          Implement the edge→cloud bridge (telemetry up, commands & OTA down) and prove edge/twin schema parity.

•          Enforce per-leaf/per-path write authority in the broker's access scopes — not by convention.

•          Validate control-grade timing and offline/failover behaviour against our acceptance criteria.

•          Work alongside our firmware/bus and 3D-UI engineers; document to our SAF-spec standard.

Required experience

•          Demonstrable hands-on work with a semantic / canonical data-model layer — ideally COVESA VSS + Kuksa, Signal K, or a directly comparable single-source-of-truth architecture (automotive signal layers, IoT digital twins, OPC-UA, or building-automation semantic models).

•          Strong systems programming in Rust and/or Node.js, with the C/C++ fluency to work close to a field bus.

•          Real field-bus integration experience: CAN 2.0B / CAN-FD plus one or more of NMEA 2000 / J1939 / OBD-II / Victron VE.Can / RV-C / LIN / Modbus / Matter-Thread / BLE.

•          Fluency with pub/sub and web-native interfaces: MQTT, gRPC, WebSocket, REST. (Sparkplug B a plus.)

•          A disciplined approach to state ownership, idempotency and determinism — you understand why discovery must never happen on the command path.

Nice to have

•          Security spine: WireGuard, mTLS, OAuth 2.0 / OIDC, Ed25519.

•          Marine, RV or automotive electronics domain knowledge.

About Safiery

•          Exports are 70% of revenue.

•          Two time nominated for Design and Excellence Award in Amsterdam

•          25+ products with in-house design PCB's and firmware in power electronics, digital switching, BMS and now 3D displays all in the open framework.

•          Aspiring to own the semantic space above the control layer with hybrid cloud in vehicles and vessels.

WHO ARE WE?

At Localsearch, we've been empowering Australian businesses with top-tier digital marketing solutions since 1993. Today, we're one of Australia's largest digital marketing powerhouses - helping over 300,000 businesses grow online.

Based in the heart of Robina, our Head Office is steps from Robina Town Centre and packed with everything you need to do your best work - including an on-site café with barista-made coffee, high-tech tools, and a genuinely collaborative culture.

As Head of Platforms, you'll lead a talented, multidisciplinary technology team, drive the evolution of our core platforms, and shape the technology strategy that underpins our business growth.

You'll work closely with the CTO and senior leadership, influencing decisions at the highest level and playing a central role in Localsearch's digital transformation - including our AI strategy.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

  • Ensure the reliable, secure, and optimised operation of Localsearch's core platforms to support business and client needs

  • Manage and evolve offshore relationships and hybrid engagement models to support IT capacity growth
  • Build and enhance vendor relationships, evaluate emerging technologies, and lead due diligence on new platforms
  • Ensure security, compliance, and data protection standards are upheld across all digital systems
  • Monitor platform performance, identify bottlenecks, and implement optimisations to enhance user experience

WHO YOU ARE & WHAT YOU'LL BRING

We're looking for a technically grounded, commercially minded leader who can bridge strategy and execution. You'll bring:

Required:

  • Extensive experience in the technology sector with a focus on software development, CRM systems, payment gateways, or subscription engines
  • Proven track record leading and developing multidisciplinary technology teams to achieve business outcomes
  • Deep expertise in DevOps practices, Agile methodologies, and digital product development

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

Nice to Have:

  • Certifications in Salesforce CRM, Zuora, or similar CRM/billing platforms
  • Postgraduate qualifications in technology management or related disciplines
  • Experience managing offshore or hybrid delivery models

WHY IT'S GREAT TO WORK WITH US!

  • Barista-made coffee daily at our on-site café - because great work deserves great coffee
  • Prime Robina location - opposite Robina Town Centre, perfect for lunch breaks or after-work errands
  • Flexible work-life balance - we know life happens, and we genuinely support it
  • Career growth & mentorship - access our internal mentoring program and ongoing upskilling opportunities
  • Comprehensive wellness program - including free access to an on-site exercise physiologist
  • Access to the latest tools & training - stay ahead in the fast-moving world of digital technology

  • Special rewards for birthdays and tenure milestones

APPLY NOW!

At Localsearch, we are committed to providing an inclusive workplace and are proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate, support, and thrive on diversity. We seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people based on merit, competence, and performance.

No recruitment agencies thank you.

HealthCare Logic is a high growth emerging leader in hospital intelligence. We provide purpose-built intelligence platforms, experiences and services to help hospital systems achieve peak performance. We empower Public and Private, individual hospitals to networks, regions, State and National, to Departments of Health. 

At HealthCare Logic we are empowering executive, clinical, and operational teams to make smarter, faster decisions that improve patient outcomes. Our flagship platform, SystemView, is embedded in and transforming over 170+ hospitals across Australia, Ireland, and the UK. 

You’ll join a borderless team of data engineers, scientists, clinicians, product experts, and customer success pros - curious minds driven by impact. Together, we’re creating a globally connected network of high-performing hospitals. 

We work at the intersection of AI, real-time data, and clinical strategy - and we’re looking for senior talent who thrives in complexity, leads with purpose, and wants their work to directly shape the future of healthcare. 

If you're passionate about data with purpose, and ready to make your mark in a company that’s scaling fast and thinking big - we want to hear from you. 

About the Role:  

This is a permanent, full-time role, fully remote and open to candidates anywhere in Australia. This role sits within the Customer Experience team and works closely with Product, Engineering, Clinical Solutions and Installations to ensure customer data is accurately represented within SystemView. You would work with our customers, shaping a national hospital intelligence platform, rather than supporting a single hospital's systems.  

You own the validation and integrity of customer data across the platform, from new deployments through to live customers. You are the bridge between how a hospital actually works and how its data is represented in our platform, translating between clinical and operational teams on one side and our product and engineering teams on the other, so that clinical and operational reality is accurately reflected in the system.  

It is a hybrid role. You need to understand hospitals, talk easily with the people who run them, and be technical enough to interrogate data, work through mapping logic, and specify what needs to change. You will work across Customer Success, Clinical Solutions, Installation, Product, Engineering and Support, with the autonomy and authority to drive data issues to resolution.  

To be clear on focus: this role is about validating and assuring the data that feeds SystemView, not configuring or building an EMR. The skills overlap, but the work here is data validation and quality, not system build. This role plays a critical part in building customer trust, supporting successful go-lives, and ensuring confidence in the operational insights.  

Key Responsibilities: 

  • Lead data validation on new deployments, reconciling source-system data against SystemView domain by domain to an agreed level of accuracy before go-live.  

  • Examine source-to-target mapping and individual data fields, defining and refining mapping logic and business rules in partnership with customer and internal teams.  

  • Facilitate validation sessions with customer clinical and operational staff, run and compare data extracts, resolve discrepancies, and provide sign-off prior to production go-live.  

  • Act as the first point of diagnosis when a live customer reports a data issue — reproducing, investigating, and determining root cause across mapping, product, source data, or expected system behaviour.  

  • Translate findings into clear, well-specified requirements that engineering can act on, and validate fixes before they return to the customer.  

  • Own mapping-change requests when hospitals restructure wards, facilities, units, or clinics.  

  • Serve as the connective owner of data validation and quality across Customer Success, Clinical Solutions, Installation, Product, Engineering, and Support, keeping issues moving to resolution.  

  • Provide Product and Engineering with clear signal on defects versus mapping or source-data issues, and feed operational insights back to inform platform improvements.  

  • Build trust with hospital stakeholders, from data and informatics staff through to senior clinical and operational leaders, explaining data behaviour in plain language. 

 What Success Looks Like 

  • Data is validated to an agreed level of accuracy before every go-live.  

  • Customer-reported data issues are triaged quickly and reach engineering as clear, actionable specifications.  

  • Fewer issues stall while waiting on unclear information or customer input.  

  • Technical teams spend less time translating customer issues, and more on building and fixing.  

Skills and Experience 

Essential  

We are open to two routes in: a clinical or health-information professional who has moved into analysis and systems, or a health-service analyst or informatics specialist with deep hospital exposure. Either way, the hospital understanding is the part we cannot train, so it is non-negotiable.  

  • A strong understanding of how hospitals work: patient flow, clinical and operational workflows, and healthcare terminology. Experience interpreting hospital data, patient movement, episodes of care, clinical workflows and operational reporting is essential.  

  • A clinical, health-information or health-service background. This may be clinical or allied health practice, health information management, or substantial hands-on experience working inside a hospital or health service.  

  • Excellent communication and relationship skills; able to build trust quickly and explain complex data issues simply to both clinical and technical audiences.  

  • Hands-on data literacy: comfortable interrogating data, understanding source systems, working through mapping logic and writing clear specifications (SQL or similar an advantage).  

  • Familiarity with hospital source systems such as EMR or PAS (for example Cerner, Oracle Health, InterSystems TrakCare, iPM or ieMR) and how their data behaves.  

  • A structured, methodical approach to validation and problem diagnosis.  

  • Able to work autonomously across multiple teams and drive issues through to resolution.  

Desirable  

  • Prior experience in EMR or PAS implementation or support, or in a health-service digital health, informatics, patient flow or health information unit.  

  • A formal qualification in health informatics, health information management, nursing or a related field.  

  • Experience in a healthtech, SaaS or software vendor environment.  

 Why Join Us 

  • Competitive base salary and benefits  

  • Opportunity for career growth within a rapidly scaling leader in hospital intelligence 

  • Collaborative and supportive team culture 

  • Opportunity to travel 

  • Flexible work environment 

How to Apply  

This is a remote role, open to candidates anywhere in Australia, with occasional travel for customer engagements. It suits someone who thrives on ownership and autonomy, enjoys working across teams, and is energised by solving the puzzle of why the data and the hospital don't yet agree. For any role related questions, please contact careers@healthcarelogic.com 

To apply please submit your current CV and cover letter. Please note applicants must have a legal right to work in Australia and answer all of the prerequisite screening questions.  

At HealthCare Logic we celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive environment where all individuals feel valued and empowered. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, including those from underrepresented groups, to join our dynamic team.  

We believe diverse perspectives create stronger teams. If your experience doesn’t perfectly match this description, but you believe you’d bring value to the role, we encourage you to apply.

jobs.job_title: Junior Firebird SQL and ASP.NET developer
jobs.job_description:
National Flooring Distributors is a leader in the flooring distribution industry, committed to innovation, operational efficiency, and delivering outstanding service to our clients. We are seeking a graduate or junior scripting, Firebird SQL & Web Developer to develop and maintain our script customisations, Firebird database systems and ASP.NET web platforms to enable smarter operations, better customer experiences, and improved support for our sales and operations teams.

About the Role

In this role, you will assist in developing, testing, and maintaining custom scripts in Ostendo, Firebird SQL-based queries and reports, ASP.NET and MVC based web portals for our internal teams and B2B customers. You will work closely with our Technical Lead to ensure technology solutions align with business goals.


Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will be responsible in the following areas:

  • Develop, test, and maintain SQL queries, stored procedures, and database-driven applications in Firebird.

  • Design and maintain web-based portals for B2B customers and sales teams.

  • Automate workflows for order processing, inventory management, reporting, and customer communications.

  • Ensure accurate, accessible, and real-time data across SQL databases and web systems.

  • Integrate supplier product data, pricing, and stock information into SQL databases and web portals.

  • Maintain documentation, code standards, operational procedures, and IT policies.

  • Prepare and submit regular progress reports on projects, system enhancements, and IT support activities to the Technical Lead / Manager.


Skills & Experience

  • Understanding of SQL development, including complex queries, stored procedures, and database management.

  • Knowledge of web development for enhancement and development of internal and customer-facing portals (C#, ASP.NET, MVC).

  • Problem-solving and analytical skills with the ability to troubleshoot complex database, web, and IT issues.

  • Excellent communication skills for collaboration with management, teams, and suppliers.

  • Knowledge of database technologies such as Firebird 2.5 - 5.0.

  • Knowledge of ERP, data integration, reporting, and automation workflows.

  • Some SQL, back-end and web development experience.

A bachelor’s degree (or working towards one) from a recognised institution in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field is preferred.

Aged Care Decisions is looking for a practical and reliable IT Support Officer to join our team in our Southport office.

This is a permanent part-time, on-site role supporting our office and contact centre teams with day-to-day IT support. The role is 4 days per week, 4–5 hours per day, on an agreed regular roster within business hours.

Candidates must have a minimum of 2+ years of IT support experience.

This role is not remote. Regular hands-on support is required for staff, workstations, headsets, phones, and office equipment.

About us

Aged Care Decisions is part of Aged Care Reviews Pty Ltd and is Australia's largest aged care placement service.

We help families across Australia find aged care options that match their needs, location, timing, and preferences. Our service is supported by a dedicated team and purpose-built internal technology that helps families during what can often be a stressful and emotional time.

We are a fast-moving business with a strong focus on helping people, improving our systems, and supporting our teams to do their best work.

The role

We are looking for someone who enjoys solving practical IT problems and supporting people face-to-face.

You will be the first point of contact for common IT support requests and will work closely with our internal IT/Product team and our external Managed Service Provider for escalated issues.

You will report to the Head of Product & Technology.

This role would suit someone with experience in IT support, help desk, desktop support, or a similar hands-on support role.

What you'll be doing

  • Provide friendly first-level IT support to office and contact centre staff.

  • Troubleshoot common hardware, software, login, printing, headset, and connectivity issues.

  • Set up and support desktops, laptops, monitors, docks, keyboards, mice, and headsets.

  • Assist with workstation moves, desk setups, and new starter equipment.

  • Support Microsoft 365 applications including Outlook, Teams, Word, and Excel.

  • Assist with user onboarding and offboarding tasks.

  • Create, update, and disable user accounts in Active Directory.

  • Perform password resets and basic access support.

  • Join PCs to the domain and assist with basic Windows 10/11 configuration.

  • Support our 3CX phone system, including softphone setup, headset configuration, and basic call quality troubleshooting.

  • Log, categorise, and update support tickets.

  • Escalate more complex issues to our MSP with clear notes and supporting information.

  • Maintain basic IT asset records, including laptops, PCs, headsets, peripherals, licences, and warranties.

  • Help document common support steps and recurring issues.

  • Support basic IT security processes, including user access, device setup, and policy compliance.

  • Work with external vendors and suppliers when required.

Who you are

  • You have previous experience in an IT support, help desk, desktop support, or similar role.

  • You are comfortable supporting users face-to-face in an office environment.

  • You have hands-on experience with Windows 10/11 desktops and laptops.

  • You have experience with Microsoft 365, especially Outlook and Teams.

  • You understand basic networking concepts such as Wi-Fi, LAN, printers, and connectivity troubleshooting.

  • You have some experience with Active Directory, password resets, user accounts, and device setup.

  • You are confident setting up PCs, monitors, docks, headsets, and general office equipment.

  • You communicate clearly and patiently with non-technical users.

  • You can manage your time well and follow through on support requests.

  • You have a practical, can-do approach and enjoy solving everyday IT problems.

  • You are comfortable working on-site in a busy office and contact centre environment.

  • You have the right to work in Australia.

Highly regarded, but not essential

  • Experience with 3CX or another VoIP/softphone platform.

  • Experience with Atera or another ticketing/RMM platform.

  • Exposure to UniFi networking equipment.

  • Experience supporting a call centre or high-volume office environment.

  • Basic Microsoft 365 admin centre experience.

  • Experience with IT asset tracking and onboarding/offboarding checklists.

  • CompTIA A+, Certificate IV in IT, or a similar qualification.

  • Previous experience working with or alongside a Managed Service Provider.

Hours and remuneration

  • Permanent part-time role.

  • 4 days per week.

  • 4–5 hours per day.

  • Agreed regular workdays and hours within business hours.

  • $35–$40 per hour plus superannuation, depending on experience.

  • Immediate start available.

Important note

We are looking for someone practical, reliable, and comfortable supporting users in a busy office environment.

Mineral Technologies is seeking a Senior Software Engineer Industrial IoT to join our Process Technology Development team.

Senior Software Developer

About the Job

Mineral Technologies is seeking a Senior Embedded Systems / IoT Engineer to join our

Process Technology Development team.

The Opportunity

In this role, you will lead the design, development, and architectural evolution of firmware and

software for our next-generation Industrial IoT and Operational Technology (OT) devices. This

isn't a maintenance role; you will be working with bleeding-edge MCU architectures,

implementing real-time networking stacks, and bridging the gap between bare-metal hardware

and modern telemetry.

You will work closely with hardware designers, interpret complex datasheets, write low-level

drivers, and build the utility and deployment tooling that brings our industrial devices to life

globally.

What You Will Do

  • Firmware Architecture & Development: Design and implement robust, multi-threaded embedded firmware for high-performance STM32 microcontroller platforms.
  • Hardware Integration: Work directly with circuit diagrams, schematics, and components to write, debug, and optimize low-level drivers and hardware-software interfaces.
  • Network & RTOS Implementation: Develop and maintain real-time applications utilizing industrial RTOS and networking stacks (ThreadX/NetXDuo environment).
  • Tooling & Ecosystem Support: Build critical supporting software, including lightweight local web UIs, Linux-based deployment tooling, and automated remote update/provisioning scripts.
  • Telemetry & Data Collaboration: Partner with the cloud and data teams to optimize edge[1]side telemetry data structures for modern OLAP data storage pipelines.

What We Are Looking For

  • Deep Embedded Expertise: Strong professional experience in C (and exposure to C++) writing production-grade firmware for ARM Cortex-M platforms (specifically STM32).
  • RTOS Fluency: Solid understanding of real-time operating systems (RTOS) concepts, deterministic scheduling, IPC mechanisms, and writing thread-safe code.
  • Hardware Literacy: Ability to confidently navigate schematics, operate lab equipment (logic analyzers, oscilloscopes), and debug directly on target hardware.
  • Systems & Scripting Flexibility: Comfortable outside of the IDE-capable of writing Python/Shell scripts, interacting with Linux environments, and building basic utility UIs.
  • Adaptability: A mindset geared toward exploring, evaluating, and integrating emerging technologies and new silicon features rather than just relying on legacy codebases

Desirable but Not Mandatory

  • Direct experience with the Azure RTOS ecosystem (ThreadX, NetXDuo).
  • Familiarity with modern data paradigms, edge analytics, or telemetry structures.
  • Experience designing reliable over-the-air (OTA) firmware update mechanisms in industrial environments.

What We Offer

  • Cutting-Edge Tech Stack: The opportunity to work with brand-new, high-performance
  • STM32 silicon and modern data storage paradigms.
  • Ownership & Impact: A highly autonomous environment where your architectural decisionsdirectly shape our global product roadmap.
  • Collaborative Engineering: A tight-knit, multidisciplinary team that values technical excellence and skips the bureaucratic red tape.
  • Benefits: Flexible working arrangements, competitive remuneration, and genuine technical
  • growth opportunities

jobs.job_description:
As Australia’s first private, not-for-profit university, Bond University based in Robina on the Gold Coast seeks to be recognised internationally as a leading independent university, imbued with a spirit to innovate, a commitment to influence and a dedication to inspire tomorrow's professionals who share a personalised and transformational student experience.


·         Work closely with the Manager, Timetabling and Examinations and the Timetable Lead while coordinating the Examinations administration and delivery

·         Be a part of a community that values creativity, innovation, and critical thinking

·         Experience a dynamic role encompassing a wide range of tasks


Senior Advisor and Data Analyst, Timetabling and Examinations (Examinations Lead)

Student Business Services

Full time, Continuing Appointment

Position Reference: 184644171


Join Australia’s top university for teaching* and enjoy staff benefits including:

·         36.75-hour standard work week

·         Up to 18/26 weeks of parental leave#

·         Salary packaging options for FBT and Non-FBT items#

·         17.5% of base salary leave loading

·         Staff wellbeing program

·         Free & Confidential Employee Assistance Program available for all staff

·         Study assistance for staff and dependents#

·         Corporate health and finance discounts

·         Onsite Medical Centre and Counselling Services

·         Onsite Sports Centre including gym, fitness classes and pool#


About the Position

Bond University’s Student Business Services department (SBS) provides administrative support and advice to students and Faculty staff on a range of matters including enrolment, timetabling, examinations, international student compliance, graduation, FEE-HELP, US Loans and Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC).

Based within SBS, this vital role will support and assist the Manager, Timetabling and Examinations, in timetabling, scheduling and examinations, including the identification and development of strategies, policies and procedures, planning, production, and scheduling of the University's centrally administered timetables and examinations. The post holder is the Examinations Lead for the team.

This appointment will coordinate the operations of examinations within the Student Business Services department, working closely with stakeholders from Faculties/academic units to ensure timely delivery of centrally managed end of semester examinations, while providing support to other Student Business Services core activities.


About the Person

The ideal candidate will be able to demonstrate the following:


·         Completion of a degree level qualification, preferably in IT or Data Analysis (or similar field), with subsequent relevant work experience OR an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training.

·         Demonstrated knowledge of university degree structures and assessment practices and the capacity to develop specialised knowledge about facilities and services of the modern classroom.

·         Demonstrated expertise in the use of timetabling software, Student Management Systems and Microsoft Office suite of products.

·         Demonstrated ability to work as part of a team with supervisory skills and the ability to roster, coordinate, motivate and train staff.

·         Demonstrated organisational skills with the ability to plan and coordinate workload, manage time to achieve goals and meet deadlines under pressure (within the context of competing priorities).

·         Superior interpersonal skills including the ability to clearly advise, communicate, consult, negotiate, and liaise with individuals in Faculties/Schools, students, and external stakeholders to achieve business outcomes.


Salary details

The salary for this full-time position commences at $99,108.89 per annum plus 12% superannuation.


Further information

Position-related enquiries can be directed to Brian Nott, Manager, Timetabling and Examinations, bnott@bond.edu.au


How to Apply for this Position

For further details regarding this appointment, please reference the Position Description and Selection Criteria


Click Apply Now and Create New Account to get started. You can:

  • Upload your Resume – your account will be created automatically, or

  • Manually enter your details to set up your account.


Please ensure your application includes:

  • A current resume

  • A cover letter addressing the Selection Criteria outlined in the Position Description above


For current Bond University staff, apply directly via People Connect, log in with staff credentials, from the Home dashboard select Careers, select the role and apply.



If you require any assistance with your application submission, please visit Applying for jobs at Bond.

 

Applications close: 23:59pm, Sunday 21st June 2026

 

Pre-employment checks: Appointment is subject to satisfactory pre-employment checks (including working rights verification, reference checks and a certified copy of your highest qualification). In line with the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Respond to Gender-based Violence 2025, preferred candidates must complete a Gender-Based Violence (GBV) Declaration, and any offer of employment will be conditional upon the University’s review of the declaration. For more information, please visit our website via this link: Applying for Jobs at Bond

 

Conditions of employment are offered under a Bond Workplace Agreement (BWA).


Bond University is an equal opportunity employer, and we encourage our students and staff to respect individuality, inclusiveness and diversity.  In line with Bond University’s values, people from diverse backgrounds, women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people who identify as LGBTIQ+, and people with a disability are encouraged to apply for positions at the University.

 

Please advise the Talent Acquisition Team if you require any reasonable accommodations to assist you at anytime during the recruitment process.


For more information, visit Supporting diversity at Bond | Bond University | Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia


Bond University Privacy Policy

#Eligibility criteria or membership fees apply
*Australian Financial Review’s Best Universities Ranking 2024

Who Are We?

Since 1993, Localsearch has been empowering Australian businesses with top-tier digital marketing solutions. Today, we're one of the largest digital marketing powerhouses in Australia, supporting over 300,000 businesses in growing their online presence.

 

Based in the heart of Robina - just steps from Robina Town Centre - our modern Head Office is built for high performers. Think barista-made coffee, cutting-edge tools, and a collaborative, energised culture where your expertise will genuinely make an impact.

The Opportunity

This is not your average data leadership role. As our Head of Data, you'll be the architect of our data future - owning strategy, governance, and innovation across the full data stack. You'll work directly with the CTO and key business leaders to shape how data drives decisions, competitive advantage, and growth across the entire organisation.

If you thrive at the intersection of strategy and hands-on technical leadership, and you're energised by building something meaningful, this role was made for you.

What You'll Do

  • Define and drive a comprehensive data strategy aligned with Localsearch's broader business objectives
  • Establish and maintain data governance frameworks to ensure data integrity, security, and regulatory compliance
  • Design and oversee our data architecture - including data warehouses, data lakes, ETL pipelines, and BI layers
  • Champion data quality through robust management practices and regular audits
  • Lead business intelligence initiatives - deploying analytics tools and dashboards that surface actionable insights
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs and deliver meaningful solutions
  • Evaluate and implement best-in-class data management and analytics technologies
  • Drive innovation by staying ahead of emerging trends and embedding new methodologies into operations
  • Lead, mentor, and inspire a high-performing team of Data Engineers, BI Developers, and Data Analysts

Who You Are & What You'll Bring

  • Extensive experience in data management, analytics, or BI within the digital marketing or technology sector
  • At least 3 years of hands-on data architecture experience across the BI stack (warehousing, data lakes, ETL, ODS, DDS, Data Mart, and Visualisation)
  • Mandatory: hands-on proficiency with SQL and stored procedures
  • At least 3 years leading and managing a team of BI and data professionals
  • Proven experience building data governance frameworks and maintaining data quality at scale
  • Strong strategic thinking with the ability to translate data into business value
  • Excellent communication skills - able to present complex data concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders
  • Collaborative by nature - comfortable working across teams to deliver relevant data solutions
  • Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in Data Science, Computer Science, IT, Statistics, or a related field
  • Hands-on experience with Snowflake, AWS RDS, Matillion, Python, or CI/CD pipelines
  • Exposure to Claude/Cortex/AI and emerging AI-driven analytics capabilities
  • A track record of driving innovation and continuous improvement in data operations

Why It's Great to Work With Us!

  • Barista-made coffee daily at our on-site café - because great work deserves great coffee
  • Prime Robina location opposite Robina Town Centre - perfect for lunch breaks and errands
  • Fun and supportive culture - we celebrate wins, back each other, and challenge complacency
  • Access to the latest tools and training - stay ahead in the fast-moving world of digital marketing
  • Flexible work-life balance - we know life happens, and we genuinely support balance
  • Career growth and mentorship - tap into our internal mentoring program and upskilling opportunities
  • Comprehensive wellbeing program covering mental, financial, and physical health - including free access to an onsite exercise physiologist
  • Employee perks galore - paid parental leave, free parking, gym discounts, 24/7 retail discounts and cashback offers, plus special rewards for birthdays and tenure
  • We celebrate success BIG! Annual company-wide conference, recognition programs, and team events to reward high performers

APPLY NOW! 

At Localsearch we are committed to providing an inclusive workplace and are proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate, support, and thrive on diversity. We seek to recruit, develop, and retain the most talented people based on merit, competence, and performance. Our goal is to represent, at all job levels, the clients we serve.

All Orbits, All Planets - Join us on our journey

Gilmour Space is the leading Australian rocket company, pioneering next-generation hybrid propulsion technologies for small satellite launch vehicles.

Are you a creative and experienced Full Stack Software Engineer I looking for a dynamic role where your skills will make a real impact? Have you ever dreamt of building rockets right here in Australia? Well now’s your chance! Gilmour Space is seeking a talented individual to join our innovative team to send rockets to Space! Conveniently located between Brisbane and the Gold Coast, our new state-of-the-art facility, purpose-built for designing and manufacturing rockets in-house, is equipped with the latest technology and tools, providing an ideal environment for innovation and excellence. So, what are you waiting for? Join us and be a part of a forward-thinking team dedicated to pushing the boundaries of space exploration and let’s send Australia to Space together!

How you will contribute to our mission in getting Australia to space:

The below is designed to give you an idea of the role you will play. There will be many more exciting and innovative things to do:

  • Contributing as part of a team of Software Engineers to deliver Software products and services
  • Participating in the development of Software engineering designs, processes and activities
  • To undertake accountability as a Responsible Engineer for Software units
  • Conduct technical assessments, including: concept studies, trade studies, analysis of COTS solutions and estimates for Research and Development
  • Adhering to and participating in product development engineering processes including: requirements definition, preliminary / detailed design, review, procurement / manufacture, assembly, integration and verification / validation
  • Supporting Software unit and integration testing and operations (may include travel to remote sites)
  • Supporting investigations, troubleshooting, modifications and fixes / repairs
  • Supporting execution of Research and Development activities
  • Supporting Software engineering production activities associated with the Launch Vehicle and Satellite product lines
  • Coordinating with the Mechanical, Propulsion and Manufacturing engineering departments as well as the Production organisation
  • Engaging with external suppliers, subcontractors and stakeholders
  • Ensuring that engineering activities are conducted and documented in accordance with Gilmour Space policies, plans and procedures
  • Other duties as requested by Management
Minimum Qualifications, Skills and Experience:
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Software, Computer Science, Electronics or Mechatronics Engineering, or a relevant qualification
  • Ability to operate in dynamic, fast paced environments
  • Demonstrated resilience in high stress situations

Desired Qualifications, Skills and Experience:

  • Familiarity developing full-stack, production quality applications
  • Familiarity with C/C++, Golang, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript
  • Familiarity with containerisation, microservices and serverless frameworks (Docker, Kubernetes, OpenFaas, Knative)
  • Familiarity interacting with RESTful and GraphQL APIs
  • Familiarity using relational, non-relational and timeseries databases
  • Familiarity using front-end development frameworks (Vue.js, React, etc.)
  • Understanding of baremetal, RTOS and embedded Linux software development
  • Familiarity developing unit and integration tests
  • Familiarity using git workflows
  • Familiarity conducting DevOps activities (managing infrastructure, developing CI/CD capabilities)
  • Familiarity with self-hosting and cloud-hosting applications
  • Familiarity developing software in accordance with Waterfall and Agile management frameworks
  • Understanding of Systems Engineering practices
  • Ability to work in a high-paced, challenging work environment
  • Ability to work efficiently in a team environment with a desire and willingness to assist other team members in the general day to day running of the department

How to Apply:

Applicants must be an Australian Citizen/Permanent Resident or have an appropriate Australian work visa or work entitlements.

If you are a proactive team player with a passion to get Australia to space and a desire to succeed, please apply now by clicking the apply button and including your resume and cover letter or head to our careers page: www.gspace.com/career

Gilmour Space Technologies is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples for all positions.

jobs.job_title: Mid-Level Full Stack Developer (Ecommerce & Web Apps)
jobs.job_description:
About the role

We are seeking a talented Mid-Level Full Stack Developer to join our dynamic team at PeriscoPe Digital Pty Ltd. As a key member of our Ecommerce team, you will play a pivotal role in shaping innovative digital solutions that drive the success of our growing business. This full-time position is based in our modern office in Burleigh Heads, Queensland.

The Role

We're looking for a mid-level full stack developer who can comfortably move between:

  • Shopify storefronts and apps (themes, Liquid, app integrations, front-end performance), and

  • Small web applications/portals that integrate with CRMs, project tools, and (increasingly) Microsoft 365 / low-code platforms.

You'll split your time roughly between:

- Building and extending Shopify and other ecommerce front ends for exceptional B2C brands

- Delivering portals and workflow tools for B2B and government clients.

Responsibilities

- Develop and maintain Shopify and other ecommerce sites:

- Customise themes (Liquid, JS, CSS), build reusable components, optimise UX and performance

- Integrate third-party apps and APIs (payments, shipping, subscriptions, reviews, etc.)

- Build full stack web features for internal tools and client portals:

- Multi-step, validated forms and dashboards in React/Next.js or similar

- Back-end endpoints and integrations (Node/.NET or similar) to CRMs, project tools, and email/notification systems

- Work with low-code / automation platforms where needed:

- Configure workflows and simple apps using tools like Power Automate/Power Apps or equivalent (we'll support you to level up here)

- Contribute to solution design:

- Help choose the right approach (custom build vs low-code vs Shopify app) for each project.

- Estimate work, flag risks, and participate in planning and code reviews.

Must-Have Skills

- 2–5 years as a full stack or frontend-heavy developer.

- Solid modern frontend skills

- JavaScript/TypeScript, React or similar, HTML, CSS

- Proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript programming languages is essential for this role.

- Practical Shopify experience (Liquid, theme customisation, storefront API usage, app integrations, responsive UI)

- Back-end experience with at least one common stack (Node.js/Express, .NET, or similar)

- Experience integrating with third-party APIs

- Good understanding of web security basics and performance optimisation

- Comfortable taking ownership of features end-to-end with minimal supervision

- Australian work authorisation is required at the time of application.

Nice-to-Have

- Experience with low-code / automation tools (Power Apps, Power Automate, Zapier, Make, etc.)

- Familiarity with Microsoft 365 stack (SharePoint lists, Teams, basic Power BI)

- Experience building secure intake portals or B2B dashboards.

- Testing (unit/integration) and CI/CD basics

About us

PeriscoPe Digital Pty Ltd is a leading digital agency specialising in ecommerce solutions and web application development. Our mission is to help businesses of all sizes succeed in the digital landscape through innovative, user-centric technology. With a talented team of developers, designers, and strategists, we are committed to delivering exceptional results for our clients.

Apply now to join our growing team and be a part of shaping the future of digital solutions!

About our Organisation 

VENTORA is the name behind iconic Australian brands including A&L Windows & Doors, Breezway louvres, Corinthian Doors, Stegbar Windows & Doors and Trend Windows & Doors.

As an established manufacturer with over 4,500 employees, our vision is to inspire our customers through leading brands, great service, innovative new products and solutions leveraging the full VENTORA portfolio, while significantly enhancing our ease of doing business built on operational excellence.

About the Opportunity

Due to internal promotion, we are now seeking our next Plant Technology Manager to oversee and manage the IT operational technology environment. In this pivotal role you will ensure that all systems are up to date, fit for purpose, reliable, secure and efficient. As the bridge between operational technology (OT) and IT, you will ensure seamless integration, collaboration and alignment of technological goals with business objectives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Oversee the implementation, maintenance and optimisation of IT operational technology systems across all business units, including multi-function devices and barcode scanners
  • Ensure seamless integration between enterprise IT systems (ERP, CRM) and OT systems (SCADA, MES)
  • Support the convergence of IT and OT environments to enable data-driven decision making and predictive maintenance strategies
  • Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment with operational and business objectives
  • Develop and enforce technology standards, policies and procedures
  • Participate in operational technology projects from conception to completion, ensuring timely delivery in adherence to defined standards and policies
  • Implement robust cyber security measures to protect assets and data
  • Conduct regular testing, auditing and assessments

About You

While ideally you will bring similar experience from a manufacturing environment, what is vital is your natural ability to communicate and work effectively with multiple stakeholders and cross functional teams.

You will also have:

  • Diploma or above in IT (ideal but not essential)
  • A good understanding of networking, cyber security and general IT infrastructure
  • Knowledge of manufacturing processes and related technology systems eg. SCADA, PLCs, MES (highly regarded)
  • Previous experience with remote access systems
  • Previous experience using InTune
  • The ability to work with and support multiple sites across multiple time zones

About the Benefits

  • Competitive remuneration
  • Flexible work arrangements, with 2 days per week WFH
  • Generous discounts on our extensive range of products
  • Ability to schedule leave every year over the Christmas period.
  • Additional paid day’s leave on your birthday
  • Flare benefits program - salary packaging, discounts and premium perks
  • Employee Assistance Program access
  • Career progression opportunities as part of a 4,500-employee organisation

Your Next Steps

Apply now to become VENTORA’s new Plant Technology Manager and help drive operational excellence!

Eligible applications must have legal rights to work full time in Australia.

Agencies will only be engaged if required. Please do not contact us or send applicants to us unless requested by the Talent Acquisition Team.

Curious about VENTORA Group? Check out our website at https://www.ventora.com.au for comprehensive insights into our operations. Prefer visuals? Watch our short overview video here: https://www.youtube.com/@VENTORA_GROUP
